## PAGE 02701: (2026x) Creating Item Flow

- page_id: `272728944`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272728944/2026x+Creating+Item+Flow
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-26T12:39:13.515+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Item Flow management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating Item Flows

The following procedure showcases how to create Item Flows whether you are working with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Item Flow management' space=''] creation mode.

To create an Item Flow

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select a Connector or an Association (in Direct mode) or an Association, Connector, Message, Control Flow, or Object Flow (in Between Part Types mode) on the diagram pane, and on its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Smart manipulator toolbar' space=''] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_item_flow.png'] .
  - Drag the classifier you want to use as the Conveyed Item onto the intended Connector.
2. In the Create/Edit Item Flow dialog, Create new or Select Existing Item Flow step, specify the following options:
  - **Item Flow**. From the drop-down menu, select an existing Item Flow or create a new one by selecting <NEW>. If you select an existing Item Flow, you don't need to specify the rest of the options in this dialog.
  - **Direction**. From the drop-down menu, select the direction of the new Item Flow. If you are creating the Item Flow on the directed relationship, the **Direction** is set automatically and is not available for editing.If you are creating the Item Flow between an Activity Parameter Node and an Action, the**Direction**field displays suggested sender/receiver elements based on the element typing the parts to which actions are allocated.
  - **Conveyed Items**. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] From the **Select Conveyed Classifier** dialog, select a classifier to be used as the Conveyed Classifier. Click **OK**. If you have invoked the **Create/Edit Item Flow** dialog by dragging a classifier onto the Connector and do not want to change it, you may skip this option.
  - (Optional) **Item Property**. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] From the **Select Item Property** dialog, select a Part Property to use as the Item Property. Click **OK**. This option is available when creating an Item Flow in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''].
  - (Optional) **Add Item Flow to corresponding relationships**. Select the check box to synchronize the Item Flow for Associations, Activity Edges, Connectors, and Messages with appropriate Item Flow ends. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the **Edit Item Flow Realization between Activities**step, review the activities.
  - If the **Add Item Flow to corresponding relationships**option is selected and multiple Activity Pairs are detected, remove the unnecessary pairs via the **Remove From Table**button. This step is available only in the Between Part Types creation mode. However, this step is not available if you are creating the Item Flow between Part Properties with no types set. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click**Review or update affected diagrams** to see the list of diagrams where the selected Item Flow can be realized and displayed. You can choose one or more diagrams you want the specified flow to be displayed. If the diagrams are grayed out, this means there are no Conveyed Items of the selected Item Flow to be displayed. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Setting project options' space=''] **Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams** controls how the diagrams will be updated for newly created or modified flows. By default, the option is set to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Item Flows whenever a new flow is created or an existing one is modified.To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click **Finish**. An Item Flow with the selected Conveyed Item (and Item Property if it was defined) is created and is set for the Connector and the corresponding relationships if the **Add Item Flow to corresponding relationships**was selected.

If you want to reverse the Item Flow direction and update it in all related diagrams, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reversing Information Flow direction' space=''].

##### Specifying the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

You can also specify a Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane if you have already created an Item Flow.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item onto the Item Flow on the diagram pane. The Create/Edit Item Flow dialog opens. If the Item Flow already has Conveyed Item(s), the Conveyed Items field is appended with the new item.
3. Follow the steps of the dialog. See the procedure steps 2-4.
4. Click Finish when you are done. [ATTACHMENT filename='set_as_conveyed_item.png']

##### Showing/hiding the Conveyed Item

You can choose to show or hide the Conveyed Item on the element realizing the Item Flow (Association, Connector, Activity Edge, Message).

To show/hide the Conveyed Item

1. On the diagram pane, select the element realizing an Item Flow.
2. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Smart manipulator toolbar' space=''] :
  1. If the Conveyed Item is not displayed , the tooltip for the button is [ATTACHMENT filename='Show_Realized_Item_Flow.png'] Show Realized Item Flows . Click it to show the Conveyed Item.
  2. If the Conveyed Item is displayed , the tooltip for the button is [ATTACHMENT filename='Hide_Realized_Item_Flow.png'] Hide Realized Item Flows . Click it to hide the Conveyed Item. If the element realizes only one Item Flow , the Conveyed Item is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.
3. If the element realizes multiple Item Flows, the Conveyed Items menu opens. In it, do the following:
  1. Select the check boxes for the Item Flows whose Conveyed Item you want to be displayed.
  2. Clear the check boxes for the Item Flows whose Conveyed Item you want to be hidden. To select all check boxes, click the **Select All**button. To clear all check boxes, click the **Clear All**button.
  3. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='Show_Hide_Flowing_Items.png']

When you hide the realized exchanges, you can choose whether you want to hide the arrow notation on the realizing elements. The **Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Setting project options' space=''] controls the arrow visibility.

To change the arrow notation visibility on realizing element when the Conveyed Item is hidden or unspecified

1. From the modeling tool main menu, choose Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, go to General > Diagrams .
3. In the Diagrams property group, find the Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing option and set it to true .
4. Click OK when you are done.

##### Realizing Item Flows

You can realize hidden Item Flows in two ways:

- Through the Item Flow Manager.
- From the selected relationship's Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To realize Item Flows through the Item Flow Manager dialog

1. Select an Association, Connector,Control Flow , Object Flow, or Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The Item Flow Manager dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='item_flow_manager_realize.png']
3. Do one of the following:
  - Set the Realize column option to true for those Item Flows you want to realize.
  - Click the Realize All button, if you want to realize all Item Flows.
4. Click Close when you are finished.

To realize Item Flows from the Smart Manipulator toolbar

1. Select Association, Connector, Association,Control Flow , Object Flow, or Message in the diagram pane.
2. From the Smart Manipulator toolbar, select [ATTACHMENT filename='realize_all.png'] Realize All Item Flows.

##### Removing not realized Item Flows

If you remove the realizing elements from the model, the no longer used Item Flows are left as a residue. However, you can specify the **Remove Not Realized Item Flows** project option to remove ItemFlows automatically after the last element that realizes them is deleted.

To specify the Remove Not Realized Item Flows project option

1. In the Main toolbar, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the General tab, select SysML .
3. Set the **Remove Not Realized Item Flows**option to *true*. The default value is *false*.

To find all not realized Item Flows, you can run the 'SysML ValSuite - Ports and Flows'validation suite. The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Not Realized Item Flow' space='VR']** validation rule will check for any residual Item Flows. 
[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Starting the validation' space='']>]]>

##### Navigating to diagrams where Item Flow is realized

To navigate to diagrams where Item Flow is realized

- Right-click the Item Flow element and in its shortcut menu, select Go To , then Usage in Diagrams , then Realizing Elements , then select the realizing element type. In its submenu, select a diagram in the displayed list.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734741 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02702: (2026x) Creating L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram

- page_id: `272734741`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734741/2026x+Creating+L1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:24.243+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L1 Node Types
- labels: ['create-l1-high-level-operational-concept-description', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the L1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram.

To create an L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram

1. Create Concept Roles.
2. Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L1 Node Types' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) P1 Resource Types' space=''] diagrams completed).
3. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">An L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the L1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram.<br /></span></p><p>To create an L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L1 Node Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams completed).</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734743 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02703: (2026x) Creating L1 Node Types diagram

- page_id: `272734743`
- space_key: `MT`

- labels: ['create-l1-node-types', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L1 Node Types diagram

1. Create Business Architectures or Nodes.
2. Connect Nodes using Node Associations.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an L1 Node Types diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Business Architectures or Nodes.</li><li>Connect Nodes using Node Associations.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734757 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02704: (2026x) Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram

- page_id: `272734757`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734757/2026x+Creating+L2+Logical+Scenario+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:26.502+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L2 Logical Scenario
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an L2 Logical Scenario diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the L2 package, and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**L2 Logical Scenario**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **L2 Logical Scenario,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the L2 package, and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='L2_drag_operational_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connecting the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Operational Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Aggregation End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Aggregation**. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.select_aggregation.png']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''].

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Relationship Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying related elements' space=''] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying related elements' space='']** dialog, select **Association.** If Operational Performers were related using the Operational Association relationship, select the **Operational Association** also.
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Smart manipulators' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Smart manipulators' space=''] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying parts and ports' space=''] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying parts and ports' space=''] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.display_related_nested.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734752 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02705: (2026x) Creating L2i diagram

- page_id: `272734752`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734752/2026x+Creating+L2i+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:25.888+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L2 Logical Internal Scenario
- labels: ['create-l2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L2i diagram

1. Model internal structures of created context elements using Node Roles typed by Nodes.
2. Associate Node Roles using Operational Connectors.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an L2i diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="74e40a9f-bcea-41a4-8f08-0d730ceaa168">Model internal structures of created context elements using Node Roles typed by Nodes.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Associate Node Roles using Operational Connectors.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733772 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02706: (2026x) Creating L3 Node Interactions table

- page_id: `272733772`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733772/2026x+Creating+L3+Node+Interactions+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:42:54.915+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L3 Node Interactions
- labels: ['create-l3-node-interactions-table', 'add-operational-exchange-in-l3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add or Operational Exchange to fill in a L3 table.

newTo add new Operational Exchange

1. In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational Exchanges by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Operational Agent , Receiving Operational Agent , and Implements cells are read-only.
- You may use an L1 view to create or modify an Operational Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new" /> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing" /> Operational Exchange to fill in a L3 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="296727d2-894a-4d95-b61a-d578e1a992f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f07942c7-23e4-4010-b873-bb1d2373f2fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational Exchanges by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Operational <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Agent</span>, Receiving Operational <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Agent</span>, and Implements cells are read-only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link">L1</span> view</span></span> to create or modify an Operational Exchange.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733756 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02707: (2026x) Creating L4 Logical Activities diagram

- page_id: `272733756`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733756/2026x+Creating+L4+Logical+Activities+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:42:53.448+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-l4-logical-activities', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the Logical Activities diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram' space='']Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram should be created first.

To create an L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9ff2594-a9e8-4506-b6f9-b31d12bef112"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the Logical Activities diagram, the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Logical Activities Taxonomy</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</li><li>Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733788 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02708: (2026x) Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `272733788`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733788/2026x+Creating+L4+Logical+Activities+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:42:57.316+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-l4-logical-activities-taxonomy', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create or reuse Nodes from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L1 Node Types' space=''] view (recommended) .
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Nodes and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create or reuse Nodes from <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L1 Node Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">view </span>(recommended) .</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Nodes and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735126 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02709: (2026x) Creating L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `272735126`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735126/2026x+Creating+L4-P4+Systems+Function+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:52.666+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-l4-p4-systems-function-to-operational-activity-traceability-matrix', 'build-l4-p4-systems-function-to-operational-activity-traceability-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (L4-P4) addresses the direct and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Implied Relations' space='']) linkage between Functions described in P4 and Operational Activities specified in L4.

To create a L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

1. Click the intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (L4-P4) <span>addresses the direct and indirect (using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) linkage between Functions described in P4 and Operational Activities specified in L4.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click the intersection between the desired elements.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733868 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02710: (2026x) Creating L7 diagram

- page_id: `272733868`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733868/2026x+Creating+L7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:07.471+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L7 Logical Data Model
- labels: ['create-l7', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L7 diagram

1. Create Information Elements or Entity Items.
2. Add Properties.
3. Create Association.

Entities can be linked with Information Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an L7 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Information Elements or Entity Items.</li><li>Add Properties.</li><li>Create Association.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Information Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733863 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02711: (2026x) Creating L8 table

- page_id: `272733863`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733863/2026x+Creating+L8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:06.069+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L8 Logical Constraints
- labels: ['create-l8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1991427608

1991427628

1991427618

To create an L8 table, do either

- Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. Select constrained Operational Element (Node, Operational Activity, Entity Item, Information Element, and Operational Exchange). Edit the Rule Specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add existing Operational Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select Operational Constraints from the model.

1991427607

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L8 Logical Parametric' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734304 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02712: (2026x) Creating layout template

- page_id: `272734304`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734304/2026x+Creating+layout+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:43.233+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Layout templates
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you create a Composite Structure Diagram and finish perfecting its layout and looks, you can reuse the same layout in other diagrams of your model by creating layout templates. Create layout templates directly from Composite Structure Diagrams as described below.

To create a layout template

1. Create a Composite Structure Diagram with Part Properties and their Ports or open an existing one.
2. Right click the Part Property the the layout of which you want to reuse and select **Display** > **Layout Template** > **Create Template**.

A layout template is created as the Class or Composite Structure Diagram depending on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Layout template creation mode' space=''] (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a layout template from the SysML Internal Block Diagram (Composite Structure Diagram in a UML project).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">When you create a Composite Structure Diagram and finish perfecting its layout and looks, you can reuse the same layout in other diagrams of your model by creating layout templates. Create layout templates directly from Composite Structure Diagrams as described below.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To create a layout template</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Create a Composite Structure Diagram with Part Properties and their Ports or open an existing one.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Right click the Part Property the the layout of which you want to reuse and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Template</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">A layout template is created as the Class or Composite Structure Diagram depending on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Layout template creation mode" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[layout template creation mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (see the figure below).</span></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_layout_template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating layout template" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating a layout template from the SysML Internal Block Diagram (Composite Structure Diagram in a UML project).</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733906 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02713: (2026x) Creating Legends

- page_id: `272733906`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733906/2026x+Creating+Legends
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:12.308+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Legends
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1294102865

1294102867

1294102864

**On this page**

**4**

1294102866

You can create a Legend right in a diagram or table where you want to use it. After creating a Legend, you also have to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating and managing Legend Items' space='']. If you want Legend Items to be created and specified for you, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Legends automatically' space=''].

##### Creating Legends in a symbol diagram

When creating a Legend in a symbol diagram, you can use either the diagram palette, or the diagram tool bar, as described below.

To create a Legend in a symbol diagram

1. Open the diagram in which you want to create a Legend.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Under the Common group of the diagram palette, click Legend , and click an empty space on the diagram pane.
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_button.png'] in the d iagram toolbar and select Create Legend .

After creating a Legend, use the smart manipulator on the Legend shape to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Legend shape with specified Legend Items in a Requirement Diagram.

##### Creating Legends in a table

When working with a table, you can create a Legend by using the table toolbar, as described below.

To create a Legend in a table

1. Open the table in which you want to create a Legend.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_button.png'] in the table toolbar and select Create Legend .

In a table, a Legend is displayed as a bar right above the table. After creating a Legend in a table, the Specification window of the newly created Legend opens allowing you to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Legend in a table with specified Legend Items.

1267814660

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the *Legends* sample model that comes with SysML v1 plugin. To open this model do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='legends.mdzip'].*
- Open the model from the <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1 directory.

36764562

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Legends' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Legends automatically' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating and managing Legend Items' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying Legends' space='']

center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend_shape.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A Legend shape with specified Legend Items in a Requirement Diagram.</h6><h3><br />Creating Legends in a table</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d64f5011-fe97-41d9-8bb1-ac65add1305c">When working with a table, you can create a Legend by using the table toolbar, as described below.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Legend in a table</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the table in which you want to create a Legend.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the table toolbar and select <strong>Create Legend</strong>.</li></ol><p><br />In a table, a Legend is displayed as a bar right above the table. After creating a Legend in a table, the Specification window of the newly created Legend opens allowing you to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating and managing Legend Items" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create Legend Items]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend_in_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A Legend in a table with specified Legend Items.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43d5d029-8ff5-4960-89a8-47b47c24ac68"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1267814660</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <em>Legends</em> sample model that comes with SysML v1 plugin. To open this model do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="legends.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br /></em></span></li><li>Open the model from the <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\SysML v1</em> directory.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bdcf196-e93b-479b-b8d2-ca5b2edf74a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">36764562</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Legends" /></ac:link><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Legends automatically" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating and managing Legend Items" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Applying Legends" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733895 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02714: (2026x) Creating Legends automatically

- page_id: `272733895`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733895/2026x+Creating+Legends+automatically
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:11.292+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Legends
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1287060642

1287060644

143669993

4

1287060643

You can create Legends automatically by extracting them from model elements. Additionally, you can automatically add new Legend items directly from the smart manipulator of the Legend already displayed on your diagram.

##### Extracting Legends

You can create a Legend with the selected Legend items automatically. Legends can be extracted either from or the .

Extracting from tagsTo extract Legends automatically from the applied stereotype tags typed by Boolean or Enumeration

1. Do one of the following:
  - in an open diagram, right-click the element shape and select Tools > Extract Legends in the shortcut menu.
  - in the Model Browser, right-click the actual element and select Tools > Extract Legends in the shortcut menu.
  - in the open table, right-click the row representing the actual element and select [ATTACHMENT filename='legends_button.png'] > Extract Legends From Row Type .
2. In the open Extract Legends dialog, select the Legend representing the stereotype tag to generate a Legend from. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

Extracting from element symbols

1. Open a diagram in which you want to create a Legend.
2. In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='legends_button.png'] > Extract Legends .
3. Extract Legends dialog In the open Extract Legends dialog, select the Legends you want to create automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract_legends_from_elements.png']
4. Click Extract .

##### Extracting Legend items

You can add the selected Legend items automatically from the smart manipulator of the Legend shape displayed on the diagram pane.

To extract the selected Legend items automatically

1. Open a diagram in which you want to create a Legend.
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend shape, click Auto-create Legend items .
3. In the open **Auto-create Legend Items** dialog, select the specific Legend item(s) to extract. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click Extract .

The selected Legend items are automatically displayed in the Legend shape, and diagram symbols are adorned accordingly.

When extracting Legends or Legend items, you can specify the following options:

- Select Create Adornments to automatically set a Fill Color for element shapes and Pen Color for paths.
- Select Connectors by Ports to automatically create Legends for Connectors depending on the selected Ports Legend(s) ( Stereotypes of Port Types , Stereotypes of Ports , and/or Port Types ).

##### Legend extraction mechanism

Legends listed in the **** dialog are generated automatically according to the types of elements displayed as symbols on the diagram pane.

| Legend | Legend items |
| --- | --- |
| Element Types | element types (metaclasses and stereotypes) displayed on a diagram. |
| Stereotypes of Port Types | stereotypes of classifiers used as displayed port types in a diagram. |
| Stereotypes of Part Types | stereotypes of classifiers used as displayed property types in a diagram |
| Stereotypes of Ports | stereotypes of ports displayed in a diagram. |
| Stereotypes of Parts | stereotypes of parts displayed in a diagram. |
| Stereotypes of Connectors | stereotypes of connectors displayed in a diagram. |
| Port Types | classifiers used as displayed port types in a diagram. |
| Part Types | classifiers used as displayed property types in a diagram. |
| Flowing Items of Connector | conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed connectors in a diagram. |
| Flowing Items of Activity Edge | conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed activity edges in a diagram. |
| Flowing Items of Association | conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed associations in a diagram. |
| Name of applied stereotype tag typed by enumeration | enumeration literals owned by the applied stereotype tag type. |
| Name of applied stereotype tag typed by boolean | true and false values. |

##### **Example**

Let's say that you want to extract the Legend identifying different **Risk** property values of Requirements in your model. To do this, complete the following steps:

1. Open the Requirement Diagram in which you want to create the Legend identifying different Requirement risk values.
2. Right-click the shape of any Requirement and select Tools > Extract Legends OR in the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] > Extract Legends .
3. In the open dialog, select the Risk Legend, as shown in the figure below.
4. Click OK .

The *Risk* Legend is automatically displayed and applied to the Requirements Diagram. The name of the element tag is used as the Legend name, and every possible **Risk** property value creates an individual Legend Item.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The process of creating the Legend identifying different Requirement risk values in a Requirement Diagram.

579958103

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the *Legends* sample model that comes with SysML v1 plugin. To open this model, do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='legends.mdzip'].*
- Open the model from the <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1 directory.

1287060641

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734092 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02715: (2026x) Creating line jump

- page_id: `272734092`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734092/2026x+Creating+line+jump
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:18.063+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['line-jump', 'none-line-jump', 'horizontal-line-jump', 'vertical-line-jump', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Line jumps represent an intersection of lines. If you have a large diagram with lots of intersecting paths, line jumps make the diagram easier to understand. The line jumps are not displayed by default.

To add line jumps

1. Right-click the diagram pane and select Diagram Properties .
2. In the Diagram Properties dialog, select the Add Line Jumps To property value from the drop-down list:
  - None - specifies that the diagram use no line jumps (default value). [ATTACHMENT filename='line_jumps_none.gif']
  - Horizontal Line - specifies that line jumps appear only on a horizontal line. [ATTACHMENT filename='line_jumps_horizontal.gif']
  - Vertical Line**** - specifies that line jumps appear only on a vertical line. [ATTACHMENT filename='line_jumps_vertical.gif']

The line jump changes are applied for the current diagram only. To change line jumps for the whole project, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Formatting symbols' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Line jumps represent an intersection of lines. If you have a large diagram with lots of intersecting paths, line jumps make the diagram easier to understand. The line jumps are not displayed by default.</p><p>To add line jumps</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the diagram pane and select <strong>Diagram Properties</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Add Line Jumps To</strong> property value from the drop-down list:<br /><ul><li><strong>None </strong>- specifies that the diagram use no line jumps (default value).<br /><ac:image ac:title="No line jumps" ac:alt="No line jumps"><ri:attachment ri:filename="line_jumps_none.gif"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating line jump" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><strong>Horizontal Line </strong>- specifies that line jumps appear only on a horizontal line.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Horizontal line jump" ac:alt="Horizontal line jump"><ri:attachment ri:filename="line_jumps_horizontal.gif"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating line jump" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><strong>Vertical Line<strong> </strong></strong>- specifies that line jumps appear only on a vertical line.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Vertical line jump" ac:alt="Vertical line jump"><ri:attachment ri:filename="line_jumps_vertical.gif"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating line jump" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><br /><strong> </strong></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8304fe09-b127-4681-a347-cbead92601b8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The line jump changes are applied for the current diagram only. To change line jumps for the whole project, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Formatting symbols" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733876 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02716: (2026x) Creating Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart

- page_id: `272733876`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733876/2026x+Creating+Lr+Lines+of+Development.+Lr+Gantt+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:08.819+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-lines-of-development', 'create-lr-gantt-chart', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create Lr Gantt chart, the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**opens.

To create an Lr Gantt chart from the wizard

1. Right-click the Lr Package and select Create Diagram > Lr Lines of Development .
2. Follow the steps of the `****` [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space=''] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the******[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Lr Lines of Development** check box.

To create an Lr diagram manually when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard** is disabled

1. Right-click the Lr Package and select Create Diagram > Lr Lines of Development .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter . The blank Lr Lines of Development chart is created.

To restore the usage of the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733913 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02717: (2026x) Creating Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram

- page_id: `272733913`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733913/2026x+Creating+Lr+Programme+Portfolio+Relationships+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:12.974+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-programme-portfolio-relationships', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources' space='']before creating the Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram.

To create an Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram

1. Create Actual Projects.
2. Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project (part) to another (whole) . Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.
3. Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources' space='']) or Actual Posts ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources' space='']) responsible for them by using the Responsible For.

You can:

- Nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read.
- Use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix' space=''] to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organization resources.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3513b26-7930-47fd-873b-7bb6a849f085"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2 diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span>before creating <span>the Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Projects.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">(part)</span> to another<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> (whole)</span>. Alternatively you can specify the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> whole and part </span>properties. <span> </span></li><li><p>Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) or Actual Posts (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) responsible for them by using the Responsible For.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="640a34e1-3325-41e0-8bbc-4d501ce74f88"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b87a6e49-5998-4084-9df0-12dd640e4b24">Nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Responsibility Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organization resources.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733892 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02718: (2026x) Creating Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix

- page_id: `272733892`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733892/2026x+Creating+Lr+Programme+to+Capability+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:10.910+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-programme-to-capability-mapping', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Lr Programme to Capability Mapping** describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.

The rows of this matrix are Projects and the columns are Capabilities.

To create an Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Projects).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix.

By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:

- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.

You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the **Specification** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Lr Programme to Capability Mapping</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability. </span></p><p>The rows of this matrix are Projects and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Projects).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix. </p><p>By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:</p><ul><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b597bf3-c2a4-4b05-bcf3-8fb746561ed2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping_criteria.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733882 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02719: (2026x) Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix

- page_id: `272733882`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733882/2026x+Creating+Lr+Responsibility+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:09.983+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-responsibility-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources' space='']D2 diagrambefore creating the Lr Responsibility Matrix.

The Lr Responsibility Matrix is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects.

The rows in of this matrix represent Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post) and the columns represent Actual Projects.

To create the Lr Responsibilitymatrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Actual Organizations and Actual Posts).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Projects).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b20a7230-c44c-4ae1-8fd6-2079374a1070"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">D2</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>before creating the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Lr Responsibility Matrix</span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The Lr Responsibility Matrix is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects.</p><p>The rows in of this matrix represent Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post) and the columns represent Actual Projects.</p><p><br /></p><p style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">To create the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Lr Responsibility </span>matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Organizations and Actual Posts).</li><li>Specify the<strong> Column Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737335 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02720: (2026x) Creating merged column horizontally

- page_id: `272737335`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737335/2026x+Creating+merged+column+horizontally
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:32.872+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Helper tools > (2026x) Tableprop tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

2050300468

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

2050300470

2050300467

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

2050300469

There are two methods that you can use to set column to merged column.

###### **$tableprop.mergeColumns(int number)**

When you put the statement in to a column, it will be set as merged column in a specified number.

For example:

**Template Code:**

| Project | $tableprop.mergeColumns($Package.size())Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |

Output:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### **$tableprop.mergeColumns(String stringNumber)**

When you put the statement in to a column, it will be set as merged column in a specified string of number.

For example:

**Template Code:**

| Project | $tableprop.mergeColumns(“3”)Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |

Output:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

See **.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The number of cells will be increased by the value in mergeColumns.

For example:

**Template code #1:**

| $tableprop.mergeColumns("5")Package |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |  |

In case there are three packages, the output will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Template code #2:**

| $tableprop.mergeColumns("5")Package |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

Output:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

2050308215

There are two methods that you can use to merge rows.

###### **$tableprop.mergeRows(Int number)**

When you put the statement into a row, it will be set as a merged row in a specified number.

For example:

**Template code**:

| #forrow($c in$sorter.sort($Class))$c.name$tableprop.mergeRows($c.ownedElement.size())#set($count=0)#foreach($attr in $c.ownedElement)#set($count = $count + 1) | $attr.name#if($count < $c.ownedElement.size()) |
| --- | --- |
| #end#if(false) | #end#end#endrow |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### **$tableprop.mergeRows(String stringNumber)**

When you put the statement into a row, it will be set as a merged row in a specified String of number. 
 For example:

**Template code**:

| #set($a = $array.createArray())#set($void = $a.add("A"))#set($void = $a.add("B"))#set($void = $a.add("C")) |  |
| --- | --- |
| #forrow($c in $a)Name$tableprop.mergeRows("3") | $c#endrow |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Note

Note

mergeRows merges only existing cells in the same column and does not increase the number of rows or cells.

For example:

**Template code:**

| #forrow($package in $Package)$tableprop.mergeRows("5")Package | $package.name#endrow |
| --- | --- |

In case there are three packages, the output will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']
