<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731164 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02722: (2026x) Creating MODAF SV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272731164`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731164/2026x+Creating+MODAF+SV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:29.197+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SV-1 is the first Systems Viewpoint view to be developed. It requires OV-2 to be completed.

To create an SV-1 diagram

1. Create Resources.
2. Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Exchange flowing via Resource Interfaces.

The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Skills (DoDAF) or Competencies (MODAF) provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the Resource Internal Exchange Specification diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create SV-4 view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SV-1 is the first Systems Viewpoint view to be developed. It requires OV-2 to be completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an SV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Exchange flowing via Resource Interfaces.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Skills (DoDAF) or Competencies (MODAF) provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the Resource Internal Exchange Specification diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><p>You can proceed to create SV-4 view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731173 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02723: (2026x) Creating MODAF SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272731173`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731173/2026x+Creating+MODAF+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:31.780+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SV-2 Systems Communications Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to complete a SV-1 before creating SV-2.

To create a SV-2 diagram

1. Create or reuse Resources created in SV-1.
2. Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Ports.
4. Specify the types for the Resource Ports.

SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the Systems Internal Communications Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to complete a SV-1 before creating SV-2.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse Resources created in SV-1.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Ports.</li><li>Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the Systems Internal Communications Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731294 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02724: (2026x) Creating NCV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272731294`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731294/2026x+Creating+NCV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:42.309+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x) NCV-1 Capability Vision
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NCV-1 are the one of the firstly created products in NAF architecture.

To create a NCV-1 diagram

1. Create a Whole Life Enterprise.
2. Add Structural and Temporal parts to the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking the Temporal Part or Structural Part button and click on the Whole-Life Enterprise body.
3. Specify the Types for the created parts (the types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements).

Additionally Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.

To fully complete the NCV-1, the NCV-2 should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. To specify the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises, exhibits relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NCV-1 are the one of the firstly created products in NAF architecture.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NCV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Whole Life Enterprise.</li><li> Add Structural and Temporal parts to the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking the Temporal Part or Structural Part button and click on the Whole-Life Enterprise body.</li><li>Specify the Types for the created parts (the types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Additionally Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.</p><p>To fully complete the NCV-1, the NCV-2 should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. To specify the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises, exhibits relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731289 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02725: (2026x) Creating NCV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272731289`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731289/2026x+Creating+NCV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:41.745+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x) NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NCV-2 should be modeled just before the NCV-1 is completed.

To create a NCV-2 diagram

1. Create Capabilities.
2. Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can also link Capabilities with the Operational and System elements using exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibitionfill in the Exhibits relationship property *environmentConditions*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NCV-2 should be modeled just before the NCV-1 is completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NCV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Capabilities.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can also link Capabilities with the Operational and System elements using exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibitionfill in the Exhibits relationship property <em>environmentConditions</em>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731301 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02726: (2026x) Creating NCV-3 diagram

- page_id: `272731301`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731301/2026x+Creating+NCV-3+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:42.967+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x) NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create NCV-3 diagram, **NCV-3 Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a NCV-3 diagram from the wizard

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. In the open window, find and select NCV-3 Capability Phasing diagram.
3. In the NCV-3 Capability Phasing dialog, click Add . NCV-3 Creation Wizard opens.
4. Follow the steps of the wizard.
5. Click **Finish** when you are done. If you do not wish to use the NCV-3 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create NCV-3”.Also you can disable the wizard. Go to **Options** > **Environment**. In the**Environment Options** dialog go to **UPDM** tab. In the **General** properties, set **Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart** to false.

To create a NCV-3 diagram manually

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. In the open window, find and select NCV-3 Capability Phasing diagram.
3. In the NCV-3 Capability Phasing dialog click Add .
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram. You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.
5. Click OK . The blank NCV-3 diagram is created.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you choose to create NCV-3 diagram, <strong>NCV-3 Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NCV-3 diagram from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>In the open window, find and select <strong>NCV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> diagram.</li><li>In the <strong>NCV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> dialog, click <strong>Add</strong>. <strong>NCV-3 Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cd4fb9a-6f91-484d-b3f4-aaeb57ff9f45"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the NCV-3 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create NCV-3”.</p><p>Also you can disable the wizard. Go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. In the<strong> Environment Options</strong> dialog go to <strong>UPDM</strong> tab. In the <strong>General</strong> properties, set <strong>Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> to false.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create a NCV-3 diagram manually</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>In the open window, find and select <strong>NCV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> diagram.</li><li>In the <strong>NCV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> dialog click <strong>Add</strong>. </li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c0fb0c0-8239-4124-87b4-555bc057a73a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The blank NCV-3 diagram is created.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731314 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02727: (2026x) Creating NCV-4 diagram

- page_id: `272731314`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731314/2026x+Creating+NCV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:45.054+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x) NCV-4 Capability Dependencies
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NCV-4 is created just after the NCV-2 is completed.

To create a NCV-4 diagram

1. Create or reuse the Capabilities from NCV-2 (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731549 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02728: (2026x) Creating NCV-5 table

- page_id: `272731549`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731549/2026x+Creating+NCV-5+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:13.174+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x) NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

924292202

924292214

924292200

**On this page**

924292204

##### Creating Strategic Actual Deployment table

The context of the NCV-5 table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating NCV-1 diagram' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the NCV-5 table are Actual Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the NCV-5 table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.

Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.

There can be one or more Actual Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To work with the NCV-5 table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a NCV-5 table

To create an empty NCV-5 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a NATO Capability Viewpoint package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. AnExhibitsrelationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Removing Capabilities from a NCV-5 table

To remove Capabilities from a NCV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An Exhibitsrelationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Exhibits relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a NCV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Actual Resources from a NCV-5 table

To remove the deployment of the Actual Resources from a NCV-5 table

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select an Actual Resource and click the**-** button. Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the NCV-5 table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

924353687

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e628c279-4224-4d8f-9a6e-7065199f1d11"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">924292202</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="148c32dd-1352-495c-943f-534292023c08"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">924292214</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="319dcbaa-baae-4e60-b5a8-a5680c6ac8bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">924292200</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49b20ed1-e42b-4773-97d3-081122454d84" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="093c6fa6-74b7-4e7b-9897-077473190d90"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">924292204</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Creating Strategic Actual Deployment table</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The context of the NCV-5 table is the Actual Enterprise phase.  The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating NCV-1 diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[NCV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_structure_relation_to_deployment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating NCV-5 table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The content of the NCV-5 table are Actual Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the NCV-5 table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5124318-7a44-4b50-acc7-4bc4bfa6ad14"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.</p><p>There can be one or more Actual Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is no longer used. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase. </span></p><p>Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To work with the NCV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="empty"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a NCV-5 table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="rows"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="columns"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Capabilities as columns in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="cells"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e729073a-7a45-4078-999a-1090c09e421d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">empty</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Create a NCV-5</span> table</h4><p>To create an empty NCV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right-click a NATO Capability Viewpoint package as the diagram owner and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt;  <strong>NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping</strong>.</li><li>Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df48fef4-8d07-4d70-8183-b442629bd475"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can click Finish in the first step of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link>. </strong>Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcaf8c6b-a839-4ebc-a4b9-75d2529195f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">rows</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add Rows </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Actual Organizational Resources</strong> dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b0cd39-cf76-49f2-a56a-f7e14c54e52e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">columns</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Capabilities as columns in the table</span></h4><p>To add Capabilities as table columns</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add/ Remove Columns </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Capabilities</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb2dee6a-f048-4967-9303-5566fa427b05"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>An<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Exhibits </span>relationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d323a01-8c43-4fb4-ab92-2179fc127d29"><ac:parameter ac:name="">cells</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears at the right of the selected cell.</li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>, select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h3>Removing Capabilities from a NCV-5 table</h3><p>To remove Capabilities from a NCV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add/ Remove Columns</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01d15d9f-1a14-49da-87e1-582976e1efce"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.</li><li>An <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exhibits </span>relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.</li><li>If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Exhibits relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>To hide Capabilities in a NCV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.</li></ol><h3>Removing Actual Resources from a NCV-5 table</h3><p>To remove the deployment of the Actual Resources from a NCV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell you want to edit.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select an Actual Resource and click the<strong> -</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="623de54d-af2e-4c4d-b0a5-0fec174f6335"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.</span> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Removing rows from the table</h3><p>To remove a row from the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a row you want to remove.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table.</p><p>The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.</p></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the NCV-5 table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deployment_report.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating NCV-5 table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="daf04d76-506e-472b-8e0c-f8b209b4d3b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">924353687</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732736 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02729: (2026x) Creating nested parts by pasting items from the list

- page_id: `272732736`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732736/2026x+Creating+nested+parts+by+pasting+items+from+the+list
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:12.184+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) UML diagrams > (2026x) Composite Structure diagram
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1877349504

1877349516

1877349506

When you have an indented list of the items you want to create as nested parts in the diagram, you can copy the list and past it in the Composite Structure Diagram diagram pane.

You can copy the list from the text editor (Word, Notepad, etc.), web page, PDF file, PowerPoint slide, etc.

To create parts by pasting items from the list

1. Copy the list.
2. Go to the diagram and press Ctrl+V.
3. In the open menu, select Elements .
4. Select the Part.
5. In the question dialog click Yes if you need to create nested parts.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The nested parts are created according to the copied list

1877349493

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating elements from other resources' space='']

ac:name="id">1877349506</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you have an indented list of the items you want to create as nested parts in the diagram, you can copy the list and past it in the Composite Structure Diagram diagram pane.</p><p>You can copy the list from the text editor (Word, Notepad, etc.), web page, PDF file, PowerPoint slide, etc.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create parts by pasting items from the list</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy the list.</li><li>Go to the diagram and press Ctrl+V.</li><li>In the open menu, select <strong>Elements</strong>.</li><li>Select the Part.</li><li>In the question dialog click <strong>Yes</strong> if you need to create nested parts.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_parts.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating nested parts by pasting items from the list" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">The nested parts are created according to the copied list</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62806c11-b9ed-4f62-9aa4-2a079b61829b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1877349493</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738652 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02730: (2026x) Creating new Constraint element

- page_id: `272738652`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738652/2026x+Creating+new+Constraint+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:26.723+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Validation > (2026x) Custom validation > (2026x) Creating new validation rules
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Examples of Constraint types

INLINE

Before creating a new Constraint element, you must decide the Constraint type to create. The Constraint type defines a target for which the validation rule will be evaluated.

There are three types of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint' space='MED'] that can be evaluated:

- Classifier level Constraints . Constraints placed on the classes, datatypes and other [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying the classifier' space=''] of the model are evaluated on all the instances of these classifiers (i.e. those [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] that have the particular classifier set as their type). Constraints defined on some particular classifiers are evaluated on the instances of these particular classifiers when validating. Inheritance is taken into account; instances of the subclasses of the class are also validated.
- **Constraints on Metaclasses**. When a Constraint is placed on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metaclass' space='MED'] (one of the classes in the UML Standard Profile > UML2 Metamodel), this Constraint is evaluated on all the model elements of that kind (e.g. if the Constraint is placed on the Actor metaclass, then this Constraint applies to all actor elements in the model). The following is an example of a rule (specified in OCL2.0) mandating that all actor names in the model must be capitalized. These Constraints are useful for specifying generic rules that must apply on all the model elements of a particular kind. text
- Constraints on Stereotypes . When a Constraint is placed on some [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED'] of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile' space='MED'] , that Constraint applies to all the model elements that have these Stereotypes applied to them. These Constraints are useful when creating domain specific profiles. When adapting UML to some specific modeling domain, a Profile is usually created with extensions for that domain - Stereotypes, tags etc. The Constraints on these Stereotypes allow enforcing the rules of that domain.

#### NOTE: Recommendation

Recommendation

It is strongly recommend not to mix the Constraints from different metalevels into one validation suite. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating validation suites' space='']>]]>

#### TIP: Examples of Constraint types

Examples of Constraint types

The examples of each Constraint type can be found in the **model validation** sample model. You can find this sample at *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features\model validation.mdzip*.

You can create a new Constraint for :

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying the classifier' space=''] , which is constrained – classes, datatypes, etc. (for classifier level Constraints). >]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED'] (for meta-classifier level Constraints). >]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metaclass' space='MED'] located in a read-only profile. >]]>

classifierTo create Constraint for a classifier or Stereotype

1. Open the Specification window of a classifier or Stereotype. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space='']>]]>
2. On the left side of the window, select the Constraints property group.
3. On the right side of the window, click the Create button and select Constraint .
4. Type its name. The new Constraint is created in the model.

metaclassTo create Constraint for a metaclass

1. Select a package where you want to store the rule.
2. Right-click this package, select Create Element > Constraint .
3. Type its name. 
The new Constraint is created in the model. If you create a new Constraint for a metaclass, you must specify the **Constrained Element** property in the Constraint Specification window. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying Constrained Element property' space='']>]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1888c6d-57fa-4208-9c47-b14398c89b83"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating a new Constraint element, you must decide the Constraint type to create. The Constraint type defines a target for which the validation rule will be evaluated.</p><p>There are three types of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraints]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that can be evaluated:</p><ul><li><strong>Classifier level Constraints</strong>. Constraints placed on the classes, datatypes and other <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specifying the classifier" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[classifiers ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of the model are evaluated on all the instances of these classifiers (i.e. those <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Instance Specifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that have the particular classifier set as their type). Constraints defined on some particular classifiers are evaluated on the instances of these particular classifiers when validating. Inheritance is taken into account; instances of the subclasses of the class are also validated.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Constraints on Metaclasses</strong>. When a Constraint is placed on a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Metaclass" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[metaclass]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (one of the classes in the UML Standard Profile &gt; UML2 Metamodel), this Constraint is evaluated on all the model elements of that kind (e.g. if the Constraint is placed on the Actor metaclass, then this Constraint applies to all actor elements in the model). The following is an example of a rule (specified in OCL2.0) mandating that all actor names in the model must be capitalized. These Constraints are useful for specifying generic rules that must apply on all the model elements of a particular kind.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8166204-806b-49f4-bc7f-90880e2dab16"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[context Actor inv capitalize:
let startswith:String = name.substring(1,1) in
startswith.toUpper() = startswith]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><strong>Constraints on Stereotypes</strong>. When a Constraint is placed on some <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Profile" /></ac:link>, that Constraint applies to all the model elements that have these Stereotypes applied to them. These Constraints are useful when creating domain specific profiles. When adapting UML to some specific modeling domain, a Profile is usually created with extensions for that domain - Stereotypes, tags etc. The Constraints on these Stereotypes allow enforcing the rules of that domain.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0304a44-7515-437e-8fa2-fb90f69773f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Recommendation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is strongly recommend not to mix the Constraints from different metalevels into one validation suite. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more how to create validation suite >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1374b10-98f0-4199-9bdb-25967b4c4c24"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Examples of Constraint types</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The examples of each Constraint type can be found in the <strong>model validation</strong> sample model. You can find this sample at <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\product features\model validation.mdzip</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>You can create a new Constraint for :</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specifying the classifier" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Classifier]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which is constrained – classes, datatypes, etc. (for classifier level Constraints). <ac:link ac:anchor="classifier"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a validation rule for a classifier >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Stereotype ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>(for meta-classifier level Constraints). <ac:link ac:anchor="classifier"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a validation rule for a Stereotype >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Metaclass" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Metaclass ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>located in a read-only profile. <ac:link ac:anchor="metaclass"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a validation rule for a metaclass >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0a5967c-2069-4c23-a9d3-437a6eddeb9f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">classifier</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create Constraint for a classifier or Stereotype</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Specification window of a classifier or Stereotype. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to open the Specification window >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>On the left side of the window, select the <strong>Constraints</strong> property group.</li><li>On the right side of the window, click the <strong>Create</strong> button and select <strong>Constraint</strong>.</li><li>Type its name.<br />The new Constraint is created in the model.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a65f2b98-de26-45cc-be32-890eb1877c18"><ac:parameter ac:name="">metaclass</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create Constraint for a metaclass</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a package where you want to store the rule.</li><li>Right-click this package, select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Constraint</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Type its name.<br />The new Constraint is created in the model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a042bc6c-0407-4f99-8b37-6ae9a060dc4a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you create a new Constraint for a metaclass, you must specify the <strong>Constrained Element</strong> property in the Constraint Specification window. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specifying Constrained Element property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to specify Constrainted Element property >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734845 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02731: (2026x) Creating new elements

- page_id: `272734845`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734845/2026x+Creating+new+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:34.354+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Selecting elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

402360606

402360608

402360607

You can create a new element directly in the element selection dialog. To create a new element, turn on the element creation mode. The Tree view is opened automatically, when the element creation mode is turned on.

To turn on the element creation mode in the element selection dialog, do one of the following

- Click the Creation Mode button.
- In the Tree tab, right-click the empty space near the model tree and from the shortcut menu, select Creation Mode .

To create a new element in the element selection dialog

1. In the Tree tab, turn the element creation mode on.
2. In the model tree, select the owner for a new element and do one of the following:
  - Click the Create button.
  - Right-click the selected element or empty space near the model tree and from the shortcut menu, select Create .
3. In the list of available elements, select the element you want to create.
4. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''] of a new element opens. Specify desired element properties and click Close .

402360605

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Selecting elements' space='']

The Tree view is opened automatically, when the element creation mode is turned on.</p><p> </p><p>To turn on the element creation mode in the element selection dialog, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Creation Mode</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Tree</strong> tab, right-click the empty space near the model tree and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Creation Mode</strong>.</li></ul><p> </p><p>To create a new element in the element selection dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Tree</strong> tab, turn the element creation mode on.</li><li>In the model tree, select the owner for a new element and do one of the following:<ul><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li><li>Right-click the selected element or empty space near the model tree and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><p>In the list of available elements, select the element you want to create.</p></li><li>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification window" /></ac:link> of a new element opens. Specify desired element properties and click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28683375-49fd-4ebe-9df1-752a0d44d1fd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">402360605</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="bd718971-deef-46d9-b3e7-e55347160787"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Selecting elements" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735450 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02732: (2026x) Creating new operations

- page_id: `272735450`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735450/2026x+Creating+new+operations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:24.317+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Specifying criteria for querying model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1325544223

1325544226

1325544225

To create a new operation

1. Click Create operation on the left side of the dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_operation.png']
2. Click the appropriate icon on the right side of the dialog to select a new operation type (see descriptions in the following table).

| Icon | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Operations group |  |
|  | Click to create a new . This icon is available only when specifying members of a union or exclude operation, input collection for a filter operation, scope and type parameters for the find operation. |
|  | Click to create a new . |
|  | Click to create a new . |
| Filter | Click to create a new filter operation and then specify both the input collection that you need to filter and the filter criteria. |
| Type Test | Click to specify a new type test operation as a condition for selected filter operation. In other cases, the icon is not available.The operation tests, whether the type of the element matches the given type or stereotype. If the types matches, it returns true, and if they not – false. You can also use this operation to check, if the element is an instance of the given classifier.For this operation, you must specify the following parameters:Element – a model element, which type you need to test.Type – a type, stereotype, or classifier for testing the element.Select the Include subtypes check box to include the inherited types, stereotypes, or classifiers of the selected Type parameter value in the test.Select the Include Custom Types check box to display the custom types extending the selected element types. |
|  | Click to create a new Property Test operation. This operation tests model elements to find out if they contain the specified property values. If the property values of a model element match with the ones you specified, the operation returns true, otherwise it returns false.To specify a Property Test operation, select the desired properties and specify their values in the Property Test specification area on the right side of the dialog.Additionally expand the operation node in the operation list on the left side of the dialog and, if needed, change the AND Operator property value. If AND Operator is set to true (by default), the operation returns true only for the elements that contain all of the specified property values. If AND Operator is set to false, the operation returns true for the elements that contain at least one of the specified property values. |
|  | Click to specify a new Nested Operation. Nested Operation is an operation, the body of which is a structured expression. It is inline equivalent to defining an executable operation in the model as an Opaque Behavior with the StructuredExpression language and then using it. Nested Operation can be used as an argument to higher order operation calls, such as Filter. In simple operation calls it is not available. |
| Union | Click to create a new union operation and then specify as many members for it as you need.You can select the Unique check box to have only one identical element count as a result.The option is especially important to return non-unique numeric values to execute mathematical operations (such as Sum) correctly. |
| Exclude | Click to create a new exclude operation and then specify both From and Excluded members for it. |
|  | Click to add a new . You can select either one of the built-in operations, which are stored in standard/system profiles of your project, or a custom operation. |
|  | Click to create a , which language can be one of the following:BeanShellGroovyJythonJavaScriptOCL 2.0It can also be a reference to a java class (Binary). |
| Values group |  |
|  | Click to select an element from the model. |
|  | Click to create a String value. |
|  | Click to create a Boolean value. |
|  | Click to create a Integer value. |
|  | Click to create a Null value. |
|  | Click to create a collection of values. |
| Other group |  |
|  | Click to add an operation executing a specified expression. Execute operation takes the supplied expression fragment and grafts it into the current expression tree for executing. Expression fragment is an XML string that can be dynamically fetched from any source, such as another tag. The unique value of this operation is that the specified expression can be calculated dynamically instead of being fixed. As a result, the calculation to be executed can depend on the . |
|  | Click to add an expression retrieving the contextual variable specified by name. Most common case is accessing the THIS variable, which stores the current . This is usually the starting point – argument for other operations – such as Simple Navigation or Metachain Navigation. |

1325551639

In other cases, the icon is not available.</p><p>The operation tests, whether the type of the element matches the given type or stereotype. If the types matches, it returns <em>true</em>, and if they not – <em>false</em>. You can also use this operation to check, if the element is an instance of the given classifier.</p><p>For this operation, you must specify the following parameters:</p><ul><li>Element – a model element, which type you need to test.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Type – a type, stereotype, or classifier for testing the element.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_type_test_updated.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating new operations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc9d9f09-8357-4b51-841c-2ecc8feb4795"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Select the <strong>Include subtypes</strong> check box to include the inherited types, stereotypes, or classifiers of the selected Type parameter value in the test.</li></ul><ul><li>Select the <strong>Include Custom Types</strong> check box to display the custom types extending the selected element types.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameters_of_type_test_operation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating new operations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="property_test.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating new operations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td style="text-align: left;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to create a new Property Test operation. This operation tests model elements to find out if they <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e3725ec1-8c54-4955-8d7d-065951d5158a">contain</ac:inline-comment-marker> the specified property values. If <strong>AND Operator</strong> is set to <em>true</em> (by default), the operation returns <em>true</em> only for the elements that <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2ab9f398-0c0e-4c0e-8fec-055c6a853bee">contain</ac:inline-comment-marker> all of the specified property values. <span>If </span><strong>AND Operator</strong><span> is set to </span><em>false</em><span>, the operation returns <em>true</em> for the elements that <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c3d2847-391b-47ee-89c4-6492f77cd0f1">contain</ac:inline-comment-marker> at least one of the specified property values.<br /></span></p><p><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="property_test_operation_properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating new operations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_nested_operation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating new operations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td style="text-align: left;"><p>Click to specify a new Nested Operation. colspan="1"><p>Click to add an operation executing a specified expression. Execute operation takes the supplied expression fragment and grafts it into the current expression tree for executing. Expression fragment is an XML string that can be dynamically fetched from any source, such as another tag. The unique value of this operation is that the specified expression can be calculated dynamically instead of being fixed. As a result, the calculation to be executed can depend on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Contextual element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_context_value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating new operations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to add an expression retrieving the contextual variable specified by name. Most common case is accessing the THIS variable, which stores the current <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Contextual element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This is usually the starting point – argument for other operations – such as Simple Navigation or Metachain Navigation.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3a7b510-9cb6-43d1-b937-91ad596c819a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1325551639</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specifying criteria for querying model" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Getting started with specifying criteria" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Simple Navigation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Metachain Navigation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Find operation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Implied Relations" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731738 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02733: (2026x) Creating new projects from templates

- page_id: `272731738`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731738/2026x+Creating+new+projects+from+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:35.857+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Creating and using project templates
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1428991632

1428991635

1428991634

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

By default, the following templates are available in the **New Project** dialog: C++, CORBA IDL, Guide to UML Diagrams Project, Java, Metamodeling, RUP, Use Case modeling, and WAE.

A new project created from the template will contain specific structure, model elements, and stereotypes that were predefined in the template. As of version 17.0.3, when creating a new project from the template, you can select to clone the custom used projects that exist in the template.

To create a new project from template

1. Do one of the following: In all cases, the New Project dialog opens.
  - From the File menu, select New Project .
  - On the main toolbar, click the New Project button.
  - Press Ctrl + Shift + N.
2. Expand the Other group and select the Project from Template icon.
3. Specify the new project name in the Name box.
4. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] button to select the location in your file system to store the new project.
5. Select the template from the templates tree and click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following table provides descriptions of the **New****Project** dialog with the *Project**from**Template icon* selected under the **Other** category.

| Element name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Other | Category | Select the Other category to see the list of the available templates. |
| Project from Template | Icon | Select the Project from Template icon to create a new project from a template. |
| Name | Text box | Type the name of the new project to create from a template. |
| Project location | Text box | Click the button to select a new project location. The Select Location dialog opens. |
| Create directory for project and related data | Check box | Click the check box to create a new folder for the new project. The folder name will be the same as the specified project name. |
| Clone custom used projects | Check box | Click to clone used projects from template project. In the new project from template, the copies of the used projects will be created. |
| Prefix to name used projects | Text box | Type the prefix for the name of each cloned used project. The default value is Cloned._ For example, if a used project of the template project is named Requirements, then the name of the cloned used project in the newly created project is Cloned_Requirements.The text box is available only if the Clone custom used projects check box is selected. |
| Select template | List | Select <modeling tool installation folder>\templates. |
| Template description | Text area | The predefined template description is displayed. Note that this field is not editable. |

You can also import the desired template into your project. Select **File** > **Import****From** > **Another Project** for this.

1428991631

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exporting projects as templates' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom project templates' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731346 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02734: (2026x) Creating new Rollup Pattern Block

- page_id: `272731346`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731346/2026x+Creating+new+Rollup+Pattern+Block
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:46.907+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x) Rollup Pattern Wizard
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1348678326

1348678329

1348678328

You can apply the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Default Rollup Pattern Blocks' space=''] or anewly created one by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='']**Rollup Pattern** **Wizard**. If you want to apply different kinds of calculations than [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Default Rollup Pattern Blocks' space='']default rollup patterns for your model, you can create a different type of rollup pattern Block. To do this, copy any default rollup pattern Block, paste it in the model, change its name, and specify all required value properties. The procedure below describes how to create a different kind of rollup pattern Block.

To define a different kind of rollup pattern (e.g.,TimeRollupPattern)

1. Find the default rollup pattern Blocks under the auxiliary resources in your project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Default Rollup Pattern Blocks' space='']]]>
2. Copy any default rollup pattern Block (e.g., CostRollupPattern). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Paste it in your model (e.g. under the RollUp Pattern Package). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. The copy of the pattern Block is created *(*e.g. *Cost**RollupPattern1*). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Rename the pattern Block, value properties, part property, Constraint Block, and Parametric diagram according to your needs (e.g., Block *TimeRollupPattern*, Constraint Block *totalTime*, properties*time*).
6. Define the Type property value of a value property in the Specification window according to your needs (e.g., from *Real* to *Seconds*). The different type of rollup pattern Block *(*e.g., *Time**RollupPattern*) is created (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1348678325

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='']

**Webinar**

- Total Mass, Cost, and Power Rollups

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figures of this page is the **Laptop Cost Analysis** that comes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='MMA']. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\simulation\LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip

## PAGE 02735: (2026x) Creating new validation rules

- page_id: `272738514`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738514/2026x+Creating+new+validation+rules
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:26.580+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Validation > (2026x) Custom validation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Managing new validation rule

927074355

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Managing new validation rule

927074358

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Managing new validation rule

927074357

#### EXCERPT: Managing new validation rule

INLINE

You can create your own validation rules in the model and use them to check your model or keep them only for documentation purpose. You can locate new validation rules anywhere in your model, but usually they are created for the classifier, stereotype, or metaclass.

To create a new validation rule

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating new Constraint element' space='']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying validationRule stereotype' space='']Apply «validationRule» stereotype.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying Constrained Element property' space='']
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying the validation rule properties' space='']
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Defining Constraint condition' space='']Define Constraint expression. The new validation rule is created in the model .

#### NOTE: Managing new validation rule

Managing new validation rule

You can include a new validation rule to your own created validation suite.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating validation suites' space='']>]]>
- How to include the validation rules to validation suite Package >>

You can manage new validation rules in your model as any other elements.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with model elements' space='']>]]>

927074354

**Related pages**
