<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731562 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02736: (2026x) Creating NOV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272731562`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731562/2026x+Creating+NOV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:15.022+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Operational Viewpoint > (2026x) NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an NOV-1 view, you can use one of the following diagrams:

- NOV-1 Free Form.
- NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description.

An NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the NOV-1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram. This approach is a bit different and may appear strange to DoDAF users, but in NAF an NOV-1 is usually created at the later stages of the architecture. For this reason we have implemented an NOV-1 Free Form diagram that is more likely what DoDAF users are used to.

To create an NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram

1. Make sure you have NSV-1 and NOV-2 diagrams completed.
2. Create Concept Roles.
3. Specify the types of the Concept Roles.
4. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an NOV-1 view, you can use one of the following diagrams:</p><ul><li>NOV-1 Free Form.</li><li>NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description.</li></ul><p>An NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the NOV-1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.</p><p>A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</p><p>Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram. This approach is a bit different and may appear strange to DoDAF users, but in NAF an NOV-1 is usually created at the later stages of the architecture. For this reason we have implemented an NOV-1 Free Form diagram that is more likely what DoDAF users are used to.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Make sure you have NSV-1 and NOV-2 diagrams completed.</li><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles.</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731576 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02737: (2026x) Creating NOV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272731576`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731576/2026x+Creating+NOV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:17.284+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Operational Viewpoint > (2026x) NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an NOV-2 diagram

1. Create Operational Architectures.
2. Model internal structures of created context elements using Operational Roles typed by Operational Performer.
3. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connectors .
4. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an NOV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Architectures.</li><li>Model internal structures of created context elements using Operational Roles typed by Operational Performer.</li><li>Associate Operational Roles using <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Operational Connectors</span>.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Operational Connectors</span>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731581 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02738: (2026x) Creating NOV-3 table

- page_id: `272731581`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731581/2026x+Creating+NOV-3+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:18.056+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Operational Viewpoint > (2026x) NOV-3 Operational Information Requirements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an NOV-3 table

1. Add existing Operational Exchanges.
2. Click the Add Existing button to select Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors. In case an Operational Connector has been selected, all Operational Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.

- You need to use an NOV-2 to create or modify Operational Exchanges.
- You can remove the rows of Operational Exchanges from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to open the shortcut menu.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an NOV-3 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add existing Operational Exchanges.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button to select Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors. In case an Operational Connector has been selected, all Operational Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c84066e-3797-4e3b-980e-804515e76528"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You need to use an NOV-2 to create or modify Operational Exchanges.</li><li>You can remove the rows of Operational Exchanges from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to open the shortcut menu.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732847 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02739: (2026x) Creating NOV-4 diagram

- page_id: `272732847`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732847/2026x+Creating+NOV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:19.958+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Operational Viewpoint > (2026x) NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an NOV-4 diagram

1. Create Organizations and Posts.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.
3. Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.
4. Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Resource Relationship.

You can also display Competencies of the Organizational Resources.

You can use the Commands relationship to display the Operational Resource that is in charge of others.

You can display Persons and Actual Persons in an NOV-4. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.

Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled just before NSV-1 view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an NOV-4 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organizations and Posts.</li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li><li>Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Resource Relationship.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can also display Competencies of the Organizational Resources.</p><p>You can use the Commands relationship to display the Operational Resource that is in charge of others.</p><p>You can display Persons and Actual Persons in an NOV-4. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.</p><p>Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled just before NSV-1 view.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732851 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02740: (2026x) Creating NOV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model diagram

- page_id: `272732851`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732851/2026x+Creating+NOV-5+Operational+Activity+Flow+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:20.541+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Operational Viewpoint > (2026x) NOV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.
2. Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.

Before creating the Operational Activity Flow Model diagram, the Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.

To create an NOV-5 diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the Diagram.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.</li><li>Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Operational Activity Flow Model diagram, the Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NOV-5 diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the Diagram.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732842 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02741: (2026x) Creating NOV-5 Operational Activity Model diagram

- page_id: `272732842`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732842/2026x+Creating+NOV-5+Operational+Activity+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:18.526+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Operational Viewpoint > (2026x) NOV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.
2. Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.

The Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.

To create an NOV-5 diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create or Reuse from NOV-2 (recommended) Operational Performers.
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between them and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.</li><li>Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.</li></ol><p>The Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NOV-5 diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create or Reuse from NOV-2 (recommended) Operational Performers.</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between them and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732761 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02742: (2026x) Creating NOV-6a table

- page_id: `272732761`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732761/2026x+Creating+NOV-6a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:12.945+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Operational Viewpoint > (2026x) NOV-6a Operational Rule Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an NOV-6a table, do one of the following

- Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the Create New Operational Constraint button and select constrained Operational Element (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Operational Information , Operational Exchange, and Mission). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add Existing Operational Constraints by clicking the Add Existing Operational Constraint button and select Operational Constraints from the model.

- You can add or edit constrained elements in every row in the table.
- You can also remove Rows (Operational Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an NOV-6a table, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the <strong>Create New Operational Constraint</strong> button and select constrained Operational Element (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Operational Information</span>, Operational Exchange, and Mission). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</li><li>Add Existing Operational Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing Operational Constraint</strong> button and select Operational Constraints from the model.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a27cb72e-1936-4e1a-bee5-a1bf21cf1587"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can add or edit constrained elements in every row in the table.</li><li>You can also remove Rows (Operational Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732777 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02743: (2026x) Creating NOV-7 diagram

- page_id: `272732777`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732777/2026x+Creating+NOV-7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:14.176+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Operational Viewpoint > (2026x) NOV-7 Information Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an OV-7 diagram

1. Create Information Elements. 
2. Add Properties. 
3. Draw Associations.

Entities can be linked with Information Elements. To relate these UAF Elements, use Association relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an OV-7 diagram</p><hr /><p>1. Create Information Elements.<br />2. Add Properties.<br />3. Draw Associations.</p><p><br /></p><p>Entities can be linked with Information Elements. To relate these UAF Elements, use Association relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731433 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02744: (2026x) Creating NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272731433`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731433/2026x+Creating+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:55.497+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Programme Viewpoint > (2026x) NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an NOV-4 before creating an NPV-1.

To create an NPV-1 diagram

1. Create Actual Projects.
2. Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project (part) to another (whole). Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.
3. Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations (NOV-4) or Actual Posts (NOV-4) responsible for them by using the Organizational Project Relationship.

You can nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read (see the figure below).

You can also use an NPV-1 Responsibility Matrix to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organizational resources.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an NOV-4 before creating an NPV-1.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NPV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Projects.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project (part) to another (whole). Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations (NOV-4) or Actual Posts (NOV-4) responsible for them by using the Organizational Project Relationship.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55e80c92-1140-43e2-96c0-3b6a2eba8cd0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read (see the figure below).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9dc6c3b9-eaab-4bdc-a188-2c1ece26cd6c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also use an NPV-1 Responsibility Matrix to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organizational resources.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731440 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02745: (2026x) Creating NPV-1 Gantt chart

- page_id: `272731440`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731440/2026x+Creating+NPV-1+Gantt+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:57.016+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Programme Viewpoint > (2026x) NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create NPV-1 Gantt chart, NPV-1 Creation Wizard opens by default.

To create an NPV-1 Gantt chart from the wizard

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Find and select NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship.
3. In the NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship dialog, click Add . NPV-1 Creation Wizard opens.
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner for the diagram. Click Next .
5. Add the project(s). Click Next .
6. Set the chart properties.
7. Click Finish .

If you do not wish to use the NPV-1 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create NPV-1”.

Also you can disable the wizard. Go to **Options** > **Environment**. In the **Environment Options** dialog go to **UAF** tab. In the **General** properties, set **Show NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart** to false.

To create an NPV-1 diagram manually

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Find and select NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship.
3. In the NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship dialog click Add .
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram. You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.
5. Click OK . The blank NPV-1 diagram is created.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you choose to create NPV-1 Gantt chart, NPV-1 Creation Wizard opens by default.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NPV-1 Gantt chart from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Find and select NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship.</li><li>In the <strong>NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship</strong> dialog, click <strong>Add</strong>. <strong>NPV-1 Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li><li>Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner for the diagram. Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>Add the project(s). Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>Set the chart properties.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb39f83f-86e7-4278-b51c-cd3e9f550975"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the NPV-1 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create NPV-1”.</p><p>Also you can disable the wizard. Go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog go to <strong>UAF</strong> tab. In the <strong>General</strong> properties, set <strong>Show NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> to false.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NPV-1 diagram manually</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Find and select NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship.</li><li>In the <strong>NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship</strong> dialog click <strong>Add</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64756969-e38f-462b-86a8-e6e793a627a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The blank NPV-1 diagram is created.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732770 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02746: (2026x) Creating NSOV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272732770`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732770/2026x+Creating+NSOV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:13.686+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint > (2026x) NSOV-1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create NSOV-1 just before finishing an NOV-2 or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the NSV-1 or NSV-2 views. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.

To create a NSOV-1 diagram

1. Create Service Interfaces.
2. Connect Service Interfaces using generalizations (general-specific relationships).

The NSOV-1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create NSOV-1 just before finishing an NOV-2 or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the NSV-1 or NSV-2 views. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NSOV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Interfaces.</li><li>Connect Service Interfaces using generalizations (general-specific relationships).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The NSOV-1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732953 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02747: (2026x) Creating NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition diagram

- page_id: `272732953`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732953/2026x+Creating+NSOV-5+Service+Behaviour+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:31.789+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint > (2026x) NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A NSOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Service Functionality Description diagram.
2. Service Functionality Flow diagram.

To create a NSOV-5 Product, you need to create a Service Functionality Description diagram first.

To create a Service Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Draw compositions or aggregations (whole-part relationships) between the Service Functions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A NSOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Service Functionality Description diagram.</li><li>Service Functionality Flow diagram.</li></ol><p>To create a NSOV-5 Product, you need to create a Service Functionality Description diagram first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Service Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Functions.</li><li>Draw compositions or aggregations (whole-part relationships) between the Service Functions.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732960 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02748: (2026x) Creating NSOV-5 Service Behaviour diagram

- page_id: `272732960`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732960/2026x+Creating+NSOV-5+Service+Behaviour+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:34.925+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint > (2026x) NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NSOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Service Functionality Description diagram.
2. Service Functionality Flow diagram.

Before creating the Service Functionality Flow diagram, the Service Functionality Description diagram must be created first.

To create a Service Behavior diagram

1. Create Service Function Actions.
2. Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NSOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Service Functionality Description diagram.</li><li>Service Functionality Flow diagram.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Service Functionality Flow diagram, the Service Functionality Description diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Service Behavior diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Function Actions.</li><li>Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732865 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02749: (2026x) Creating NSV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272732865`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732865/2026x+Creating+NSV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:21.998+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) NSV-1 System Interface Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

NSV-1 is the first Systems Viewpoint view to be developed. It requires NOV-2 to be completed.

To create an NSV-1 diagram

1. Create Resources.
2. Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Interfaces.

The NSV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Competencies provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the **System Interface Internal Description** diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create NSV-4 view as soon as the NSV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>NSV-1 is the first Systems Viewpoint view to be developed. It requires NOV-2 to be completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NSV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Interfaces.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The NSV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Competencies provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the <strong>System Interface Internal Description</strong> diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><p>You can proceed to create NSV-4 view as soon as the NSV-1 has been completed.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732958 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02750: (2026x) Creating NSV-10a table

- page_id: `272732958`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732958/2026x+Creating+NSV-10a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:33.751+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) NSV-10a Systems Rule Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a NSV-10a table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the Create New Resource Constraint button and select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange), and then fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add the Existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing Resource Constraint button and selecting Resource Constraints .

- You can add and edit constrained elements for every Row in the table.
- You can remove Rows (Resource Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a NSV-10a table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the <strong>Create New Resource Constraint</strong> button and select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange), and then fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</li><li>Add the Existing Resource Constraints by clicking the<strong> Add Existing Resource Constraint</strong> button and selecting <strong>Resource Constraints</strong>.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba8cb062-0adf-4a6b-86cb-48719f06dc29"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can add and edit constrained elements for every Row in the table.</li><li>You can remove Rows (Resource Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732881 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02751: (2026x) Creating NSV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272732881`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732881/2026x+Creating+NSV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:23.354+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) NSV-2 System Communications Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to complete a NSV-1 before creating NSV-2.

To create a NSV-2 diagram

1. Create or reuse Resources created in NSV-1.
2. Associate Resources using Resource interfaces.
3. Create Resource Ports.
4. Specify the types for the Resource Ports.

NSV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the **System Internal Communications Description** diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to complete a NSV-1 before creating NSV-2.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NSV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse Resources created in NSV-1.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Ports.</li><li>Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>NSV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the <strong>System Internal Communications Description</strong> diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730129 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02752: (2026x) Creating NSV-4 Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `272730129`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730129/2026x+Creating+NSV-4+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:34.149+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) NSV-4 System Functionality Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create NSV-4, make sure there are NOV-5 and NSV-1 created in the architecture.

The NSV-4 view consists of two diagrams:

1. Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.
2. Functionality Description Flow diagram.This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.

You must create the Functionality Description diagram before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram.

To create a NSV-4 Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from NSV-1, NSV-2.
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between the Resources and Functions.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between the Functions if necessary.
5. Draw an Implements relationship between the Functions and Operational Activities from NOV-5.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create NSV-4, make sure there are NOV-5 and NSV-1 created in the architecture.</p><p>The NSV-4 view consists of two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.</li><li>Functionality Description Flow diagram.This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.</li></ol><p>You must create the Functionality Description diagram before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NSV-4 Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from NSV-1, NSV-2.</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between the Resources and Functions.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Draw an Implements relationship between the Functions and Operational Activities from NOV-5.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730159 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02753: (2026x) Creating NSV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram

- page_id: `272730159`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730159/2026x+Creating+NSV-4+Functionality+Description+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:36.080+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) NSV-4 System Functionality Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create NSV-4, ensure NOV-5 and NSV-1 are created in the architecture.

The NSV-4 view consists of two diagrams:

1. Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.
2. Functionality Description Flow diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.

Before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram, the Functionality Description diagram must be created first.

To create a NSV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.
3. Display the possible Resource Exchange s on every Function Edge.

To display possible Resource Exchanges on selected Activity Edge, click smart manipulator named “Resource Exchange” that appears when Function Edge is selected. You will be able to select possible to realize Resource Exchange. Another way to invoke the Resource Exchange selection Dialog is to click on the “Resource Exchange” button in the Diagram toolbar and to click on the Function Edge.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create NSV-4, ensure NOV-5 and NSV-1 are created in the architecture.</p><p>The NSV-4 view consists of two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.</li><li>Functionality Description Flow diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram, the Functionality Description diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NSV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.</li><li>Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Resource Exchange s on every Function Edge.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To display possible Resource Exchanges on selected Activity Edge, click smart manipulator named “Resource Exchange” that appears when Function Edge is selected. You will be able to select possible to realize Resource Exchange. Another way to invoke the Resource Exchange selection Dialog is to click on the “Resource Exchange” button in the Diagram toolbar and to click on the Function Edge.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730175 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02754: (2026x) Creating NSV-8 diagram

- page_id: `272730175`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730175/2026x+Creating+NSV-8+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:37.366+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) NSV-8 System Evolution Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SV-8 diagram

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Find and select NSV-8 System Evolution Description.
3. In the NSV-8 Capability Configuration Management dialog click Add .
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram. You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.
5. Click OK . The blank NSV-8 diagram is created.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a SV-8 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Find and select NSV-8 System Evolution Description.</li><li>In the <strong>NSV-8 Capability Configuration Management</strong> dialog click <strong>Add</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6ccb543-8f7c-4b16-bb77-e212475c1c17"><ac:rich-text-body>You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The blank NSV-8 diagram is created.</li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730239 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02755: (2026x) Creating NSV-9 table

- page_id: `272730239`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730239/2026x+Creating+NSV-9+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:42.522+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) NSV-9 Technology Forecast
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658547230

1658547244

1658547233

To create a NSV-9 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658547228

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730234 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02756: (2026x) Creating NTV-1 table

- page_id: `272730234`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730234/2026x+Creating+NTV-1+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:41.989+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Technical Viewpoint > (2026x) NTV-1 Technical Standards Profile
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a NTV-1 table, do either

- Add a new UAF Element by clicking the Add new UAF Element button and select any UAF Element (this term means any of the UAF available elements). Specify the owner for the selected element.
- Add the Existing UAF elements by clicking the Add Existing UAF Element button and select the UAF elements.
- Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UAF element must conform to by clicking the … button on any cell in the Standard/Policy column.

- You can remove Rows (UAF Elements) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a NTV-1 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new <span>UAF </span>Element by clicking the <strong>Add new UAF Element</strong> button and select any <span>UAF </span>Element (this term means any of the <span>UAF </span>available elements). Specify the owner for the selected element.</li><li>Add the Existing <span>UAF </span>elements by clicking the<strong> Add Existing UAF Element</strong> button and select the <span>UAF </span>elements.</li><li>Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular <span>UAF </span>element must conform to by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on any cell in the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6c1ef35-9cd3-4f6b-b63e-99fa250ea91d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can remove Rows (UAF Elements) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739266 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02757: (2026x) Creating NTV-2 table

- page_id: `272739266`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739266/2026x+Creating+NTV-2+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:10.426+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Technical Viewpoint > (2026x) NTV-2 Technical Standards Forecast
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658580680

1658580694

1658580683

To create an NTV-2 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658580678

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734747 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02758: (2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram

- page_id: `272734747`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734747/2026x+Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+L1+Node+Types+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:25.117+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L1 Node Types
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-l1-node-types', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the L1 Node Types diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Associationbetween a pair of Nodes and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, select the Association.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

An L1 Node Types diagram also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Nodes, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Nodes.

The structure of each Node can be modeled using the Logical Scenario diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

Once the L1 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L4 Logical Activities' space=''] diagram.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 02760: (2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram

- page_id: `272733777`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733777/2026x+Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+L4+Logical+Activities+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:42:55.915+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-l4-logical-activities', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the L4 Logical Activities diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange**wizard . To open the wizard, do either:
  - On the diagram pane, select an Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow between a pair of Operational Activities and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_operational_exchange.png'] .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange.png'] and on the diagram pane, select an Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731574 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02762: (2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in NOV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272731574`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731574/2026x+Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+NOV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:16.957+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Operational Viewpoint > (2026x) NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the NOV-2 diagram

1. Open the Operational Exchange creation wizard. To open the wizard, do either:
  - Select a Operational Connector and on the smart manipulator, click the New Operational Exchange button.
  - On the Operational Exchanges toolbar in the diagram palette, click the Operational Exchange button and then click the Operational Connector.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange item either by choosing an existing Operational Exchange or creating a new one.
3. Specify the direction of the Operational Exchange.

An NOV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Location requirements, and Operational Ports provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the Operationa Node Internal Relationship Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

Once the NOV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the NOV-5 diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an Operational Exchange in the NOV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> creation wizard. To open the wizard, do either:<ul><li>Select a <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Operational Connector </span>and on the smart manipulator, click the <strong>New Operational Exchange</strong> button.</li><li>On the <strong>Operational Exchanges</strong> toolbar in the diagram palette, click the <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> button and then click the Operational Connector.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange item either by choosing an existing Operational Exchange or creating a new one.</li><li>Specify the direction of the Operational Exchange.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>An NOV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Location requirements, and Operational Ports provided or requested by Operational Performers.</p><p>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the Operationa Node Internal Relationship Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><p>Once the NOV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the NOV-5 diagram.</p>
````

- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-ov-2', 'create-operational-exchange-in-operational-resource-flow-description', 'msosa']

To create an Operational Exchange in the OV-2 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Associationbetween a pair of Operational Performers and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739794 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02768: (2026x) Creating OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram

- page_id: `272739794`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739794/2026x+Creating+OV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Graphic+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:58.296+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Operational viewpoint > (2026x) OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic
- labels: ['create-ov-1-high-level-operational-concept-graphic-diagram', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the OV-1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

To create an OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram

1. Create Concept Roles.
2. Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] diagrams completed).
3. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the OV-1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.</p><p>A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams completed).</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739767 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02769: (2026x) Creating OV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272739767`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739767/2026x+Creating+OV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:54.115+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Operational viewpoint > (2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-ov-2-operational-resource-flow-description', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

To create an OV-2 diagram

1. Create Operational Performers.
2. Connect them using the Associations relationship.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Association.

An OV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-2 Internal diagram' space=''] diagram.

Once the OV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="844e8454-fb00-4760-b817-89cb9bc6b387"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create an OV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Performers.</li><li>Connect them using the Associations relationship.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Association.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>An OV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.</span></p><p>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-2 Internal diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Internal Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><p>Once the OV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p> </p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739771 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02770: (2026x) Creating OV-2 Internal diagram

- page_id: `272739771`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739771/2026x+Creating+OV-2+Internal+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:55.646+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Operational viewpoint > (2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Internal Description
- labels: ['create-ov-2-operational-resource-flow-internal-description', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The owner of the OV-2 Internal diagram must be Operational Performer.

To create an OV-2 Internal diagram

1. Create Operational Roles.
2. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connectors .
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab520339-ec3a-4ea9-ac87-ad3df225ba23"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The owner of the OV-2 Internal diagram must be Operational Performer.</p><p>To create an OV-2 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Roles.</li><li>Associate Operational Roles using <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Connectors</span>.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Connectors</span>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

## PAGE 02771: (2026x) Creating OV-3 role-based table

- page_id: `272739773`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739773/2026x+Creating+OV-3+role-based+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:55.859+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Operational viewpoint > (2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational Agents). You can add anexisting Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the OV-3 role-based table.

To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the OV-3 role-based table

1. In the OV-3 role-based diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector, and click **+** button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Agents</span>). You can add <span class="confluence-link">an </span><span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the OV-3 role-based table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the OV-3 role-based table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the OV-3 role-based diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p>Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3f29e07-4f97-4eae-9177-b78a40adfed2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For multiple selection, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector, and click <strong>+</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739775 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02772: (2026x) Creating OV-3 table

- page_id: `272739775`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739775/2026x+Creating+OV-3+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:55.925+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Operational viewpoint > (2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: ['add-operational-exchange-in-ov-3', 'create-ov-3-table', 'create-operational-exchange-in-ov-3-operational-resource-flow-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add new or existing Operational Exchange to fill in an OV-3 table.

To add new Operational Exchange

1. In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

To add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational Exchanges by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Operational Agent, Receiving Operational Agent, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']OV-2 view to create or modify an Operational Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchange to fill in an OV-3 table.</p><p>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational Exchanges by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Sending Operational Agent, Receiving <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Agent</span>, and Implements cells are read only.</span></li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-2</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> view</span></span> to create or modify an Operational Exchange.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739777 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02773: (2026x) Creating OV-4 diagram

- page_id: `272739777`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739777/2026x+Creating+OV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:55.996+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Operational viewpoint > (2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart
- labels: ['create-ov-4-organizational-relationship-chart', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Typical and Actual organizational relationships are modeled in OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart.

To create an OV-4 Typical diagram

1. Create Organizations, Posts, andPersons.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.

To create an OV-4 Actual diagram

1. Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.
2. Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship . [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_4_actual.png']

You can display:

- the Competence of the Organizational Resources.
- the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.
- the Resources and in general they could be modeled just before [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Typical and Actual organizational relationships are modeled in OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart.</p><p>To create an OV-4 Typical diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Organizations, </span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Posts, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">and </span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Persons</span></span>.</span></span></li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To create an OV-4 Actual diagram<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Organizations, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Posts, and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Persons</span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Relate Actual Organizational Resources with <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Organization Relationship</span></span>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="OV-4 Actual" ac:alt="OV-4 Actual"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ov_4_actual.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-4 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="439ad953-f2b7-48c9-9049-4b629719adf6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Competence </span></span>of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.</li><li>the Resources and in general they could be modeled just before <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````
