<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731956 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02774: (2026x) Creating OV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model diagram

- page_id: `272731956`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731956/2026x+Creating+OV-5+Operational+Activity+Flow+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:55.106+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Operational Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. OV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The OV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.
2. Operational Activity Flow Model.This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.

Before creating the Operational Activity Flow Model diagram, the Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.

To create an OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the Diagram.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The OV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.</li><li>Operational Activity Flow Model.This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Operational Activity Flow Model diagram, the Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the Diagram.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732149 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02775: (2026x) Creating OV-5 Operational Activity Model diagram

- page_id: `272732149`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732149/2026x+Creating+OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:18.530+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Operational Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. OV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The OV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.
2. Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.

The Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.

To create an OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create or Reuse from OV-2 (recommended) Operational Performers.
3. Draw Is Capable to Perform relationship between them and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The OV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.</li><li>Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.</li></ol><p>The Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create or Reuse from OV-2 (recommended) Operational Performers.</li><li>Draw Is Capable to Perform relationship between them and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol>
````

- labels: ['create-ov-5a-operational-activity-model', 'msosa']

The Operational Activity Model represents Operational Activity hierarchies. It must be created first.

To create an OV-5a diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create Operational Performers or reuse them from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] (recommended) .
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Operational Performers and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.


- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739852/2026x+Creating+OV-5b+Operational+Activity+Flow+Model+diagram

- labels: ['create-ov-5b-operational-activity-flow-model', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Operational Activity Flow Model represents Operational Activity flows. Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram' space=''] must be created first.

To create an OV-5b diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions wit h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739856 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02778: (2026x) Creating OV-6a table

- page_id: `272739856`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739856/2026x+Creating+OV-6a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:05.957+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Operational viewpoint > (2026x) OV-6a Operational Rules Model
- labels: ['add-operational-constraint-in-ov-6a', 'create-ov-6a-operational-rules-model', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an OV-6a table, do one of the following

- Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the Add New button. The new row is added in table. Fill in three columns: 
- Applies To . Select the cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] . In the Select Subject Of Operational Constraint dialog, select constrained Operational Elements (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Information Element, Operational Exchange). 
- **Rule****Specification**. Select the cell and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. In the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify body with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL). 
- **Rule Kind**. Click the cell and from drop-down list select the rule kind.
- Add an existing Operational Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button. In Select Operational Constraint dialog, select Operational Constraints. The filled row is added in table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an OV-6a table, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> button. The new row is added in table. Fill in three columns:<br />-  </span><strong>Applies To</strong>. Select the cell and click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. In the <strong>Select Subject Of Operational Constraint</strong> dialog, select constrained Operational Elements (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Information Element, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Operational Exchange).<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- <strong style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Rule </strong><strong>Specification</strong>. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select the cell</span> and click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>. In the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog, select the language and specify body with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).<br />- <strong>Rule Kind</strong>. Click the cell and from drop-down list select the rule kind.<br /></span></span></li><li>Add an existing Operational Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. In <strong>Select Operational Constraint</strong> dialog, select Operational Constraints.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> The filled row is added in table. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><br /><span> </span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /> </span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734999 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02779: (2026x) Creating P1 Resource Types diagram

- page_id: `272734999`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734999/2026x+Creating+P1+Resource+Types+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:42.389+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-resource-types', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P1 Resource Types diagram

1. Create Resources.
2. Connect Resources using Resource Associations.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Associations.

The P1 Resource Types diagram also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Competences provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) P2 Resource Internal Structure' space=''] diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) P4 Resource Functions' space=''] view as soon as the P1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a P1 Resource Types diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources.</li><li>Connect Resources using Resource Associations.</li><li>Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Associations.</li></ol><p>The P1 Resource Types diagram also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Competences provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P2 Resource Internal Structure" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45fbb702-bd2a-4e42-9ae9-b6702a7e6257"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P4 Resource Functions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the P1 has been completed.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735005 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02780: (2026x) Creating P1 Service Provision matrix

- page_id: `272735005`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735005/2026x+Creating+P1+Service+Provision+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:42.844+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-service-provision-matrix', 'build-p1-service-provision-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows in a P1 Service Provision matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Resources.

To create a P1 Service Provision matrix

1. Specify the Rows scope (Service Interfaces).
2. Specify the Columns scope (Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

The cells in the matrix represent a Resource Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The rows in a P1 Service Provision matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Resources.</p><p>To create a P1 Service Provision matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Rows scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns scope</strong> (Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>The cells in the matrix represent a Resource Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735001 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02781: (2026x) Creating P1 Systems to Systems Matrix

- page_id: `272735001`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735001/2026x+Creating+P1+Systems+to+Systems+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:42.532+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-systems-to-systems-matrix', 'build-p1-systems-to-systems-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems to Systems Matrix (P1)****shows how resources interact with each otherdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using Implied Relations' space=''].A P1 Systems to Systems Matrix is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.

Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. The P1 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To create a P1 Systems to Systems matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (System Resources).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Systems to Systems Matrix (P1)<strong> </strong><span>shows how resources interact with each other </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>A P1 Systems to Systems Matrix is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. The P1 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a P1 Systems to Systems matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735007 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02782: (2026x) Creating P1 Technology Forecast table

- page_id: `272735007`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735007/2026x+Creating+P1+Technology+Forecast+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:43.122+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-technology-forecast-table', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658799737

1658799749

1658799738

To create a P1 Technology Forecast table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658799736

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735013 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02783: (2026x) Creating P2 Structure diagram

- page_id: `272735013`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735013/2026x+Creating+P2+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:43.616+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P2 Resource Structure
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### topCreating a diagram

To create a P2 Resource Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the P2 package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**P2 Resource Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **P2****Resource Structure,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Resource Artifact element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create a Resource Artifact in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Resource Artifact and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Resource Artifact in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Resource Structure package, and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Resource Artifact and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='P2_drag_resource_artifact.png']

Resource Artifact

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Resource Artifact (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connecting the Resource Artifact

When you have Resource Artifact created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Resource Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Aggregation End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element, which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''].

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose to use this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose to use this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose to use this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying related elements' space=''] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying related elements' space='']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Smart manipulators' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Smart manipulators' space=''] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying parts and ports' space=''] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying parts and ports' space=''] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.display_related_nested.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

/><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abe3734a-44b5-4d45-8a04-40ea23304a35"><ac:rich-text-body>If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the <strong>Resource Association</strong> also.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.</li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="891" ac:width="740"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.display_related.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating P2 Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.compartments.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating P2 Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="879f1986-4eb4-464d-879b-270ccfa1b21e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.attributes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating P2 Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="722" ac:width="756"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.display_related_nested.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating P2 Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735043 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02784: (2026x) Creating P2i diagram

- page_id: `272735043`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735043/2026x+Creating+P2i+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:45.306+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P2 Resource Internal Structure
- labels: ['create-p2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P2i diagram

1. Create Resources Roles or drag Resources from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.
2. Specify the types of the Resource Roles.
3. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors or Resource Interfaces.
4. Create Resource Ports.
5. Specify the types for the Resource Ports.

A P2i diagram allows to show the provided and required services by the particular Resource Roles.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create a P2i diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resources Roles or drag Resources from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify the types of the Resource Roles.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors or Resource Interfaces.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resource Ports.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">A P2i diagram allows to show the provided and required services by the particular Resource Roles.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735049 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02785: (2026x) Creating P3 table

- page_id: `272735049`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735049/2026x+Creating+P3+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:45.690+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P3 Resource Connectivity
- labels: ['create-p3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add or Resource Exchangesto fill in a P3 table.

newTo add new Resource Exchange

1. In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Resource Exchange

1. In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Resource Exchanges by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) P1 Resource Types' space=''] view to create or modify a Resource Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new" /> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing" /> Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>to fill in a P3 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="296727d2-894a-4d95-b61a-d578e1a992f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>are added to the table.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f07942c7-23e4-4010-b873-bb1d2373f2fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Select Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>Identifier, Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view</span></span> to create or modify a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735122 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02786: (2026x) Creating P4 Resource Functions diagram

- page_id: `272735122`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735122/2026x+Creating+P4+Resource+Functions+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:51.542+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-p4-resource-functions', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the P4 Resource Functions diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram' space=''] should be created first.

To create a P4 Resource Functions diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
3. Display the possible Resource Exchanges on every Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

To display possible ResourceExchanges, do either

- Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
- In the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6105221-2fb5-4b1d-9268-05a0995eb683"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the P4 Resource Functions diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a P4 Resource Functions diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</li><li>Display the possible Resource Exchanges on every Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71af54d1-3d83-4531-b4de-b16ed860afc7"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">To display possible Resource </span>Exchanges<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">, do either<br /></span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Select Function </span>Control Flow or Function Object Flow and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange" ac:alt="New Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the diagram palette, click <ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange" ac:alt="Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" opens.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735120 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02787: (2026x) Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `272735120`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735120/2026x+Creating+P4+Resource+Functions+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:51.294+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-p4-resource-functions-taxonomy', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) P1 Resource Types' space=''] view.
3. Use Is Capable To Perform relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L4 Logical Activities' space=''] view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from<span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></li><li>Use Is Capable To Perform relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L4 Logical Activities" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735131 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02788: (2026x) Creating P7 diagram

- page_id: `272735131`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735131/2026x+Creating+P7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:53.662+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P7 Physical Data Model
- labels: ['create-p7', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P7 diagram

1. Create Data Elements.
2. Add Properties for Data Elements.
3. Draw Association of Information relationships between Data Elements.

Entities can be linked with Data Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a P7 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Data Elements.</li><li>Add Properties for Data Elements.</li><li>Draw Association of Information relationships between Data Elements.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Data Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735143 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02789: (2026x) Creating P8 table

- page_id: `272735143`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735143/2026x+Creating+P8+table
- version_number: 1

- labels: ['create-p8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P8 table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the **Add New** button. The new row in the table is created.Select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Data Element, Entity Item, Resource Exchange ). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add the existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select existing Resource Constraints from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ce018c8-0c16-450f-b89b-b2c7c9909dd7"> S</ac:inline-comment-marker></span>elect a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, </span>Data Element, Entity Item, Resource Exchange<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</span></li><li>Add the existing Resource Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong> button and select existing Resource Constraints from the model.</li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739819 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 02790: (2026x) Creating path variables

- page_id: `272739819`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739819/2026x+Creating+path+variables
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-03-17T15:24:22.330+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The main purpose of the path variables feature is to give additional sharing flexibility. It enables you to store paths in your project as named variables that can be easily adjusted on different machines.

Path Variables may be used in:

- Code engineering classpath
- Working directories
- Hyperlinks
- Modules paths
- Reports

To create a path variable

1. In the main menu, go to Options > Environment . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog opens.
2. On the left part of the dialog, select Path Variables .
3. In the Path Variables area click the Add button. The Path Variable dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='environment_options_dialog.png']
4. In the appropriate boxes enter the path variable name and description.
5. Next to the Value box, click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] , navigate to the desired location, and click Open .
6. In the Path Variable dialog, click OK . Your custom path variable is created and shown in the Defined Path Variables list.
7. In the Environment Options dialog, click OK .

Every user can specify different values of the same path variables. However, path variables should be resolved only on demand, when they are used. If a modeling tool determines you can use path variables, once you specify your absolute path, the application automatically opens the **Use Path Variables** dialog box. For example, this dialog may open when in your project, you try to use another project **From a predefined location** and add a new path to the **Paths to used projects** list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you do not want your modeling tool to automatically display the **Use Path Variables** dialog box, clear the **Show suggestion to use path variables** check box. To use the original path and cancel this dialog, click the **Use Original** button.

To use a suggested path variable

- In the **Available forms** list, select a path and click the **Use Selected** button.

If you have your own documentation running on the local server, you can define the path in the *documentation.server* option.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The main purpose of the path variables feature is to give additional sharing flexibility. It enables you to store paths in your project as named variables that can be easily adjusted on different machines.</p><p>Path Variables may be used in:</p><ul><li data-uuid="451c6457-1736-4479-baf4-914a23cfd554"><span>Code engineering classpath</span></li><li class="p" data-uuid="18183aae-cbf2-405a-8e78-bf5e27e57ecb"><p>Working directories</p></li><li class="p" data-uuid="4e2a4c10-03c8-4379-b161-ad2913d2320f"><p>Hyperlinks</p></li><li class="p" data-uuid="c3314e03-c9e5-4d3a-917a-b5d8da174de0"><p>Modules paths</p></li><li class="p" data-uuid="300af836-40ec-4c32-9062-826fe186c1e7"><p>Reports</p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create a path variable</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9e2680c1-92ae-4010-b2f3-92c82298213f">In the main menu, go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li data-uuid="ff204c61-4701-4dca-a691-91c3b49b49de">On the left part of the dialog, select <strong>Path Variables</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="3fcda674-4e73-4dfe-b24b-d11a613dbd29">In the <strong>Path Variables</strong> area click the <strong>Add</strong> button. The <strong>Path Variable</strong> dialog opens.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_options_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="a289c4fc-4c4e-48e6-98ef-943e0ff105c5">In the appropriate boxes enter the path variable name and description.</li><li data-uuid="5a898b0a-9d55-481a-9898-8051a4d45c46">Next to the <strong>Value</strong> box, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png" /></ac:image>, navigate to the desired location, and click <strong>Open</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="a09e4d90-d7b7-471a-b710-23b9191a02b7">In the <strong>Path Variable</strong> dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. Your custom path variable is created and shown in the <strong>Defined Path Variables</strong> list.</li><li data-uuid="33595dad-01c4-48e9-9940-a4e6fe4281e6">In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /> </li></ol><p>Every user can specify different values of the same path variables. However, path variables should be resolved only on demand, when they are used. If a modeling tool determines you can use path variables, once you specify your absolute path, the application automatically opens the <strong>Use Path Variables</strong> dialog box. For example, this dialog may open when in your project, you try to use another project <strong>From a predefined location</strong> and add a new path to the <strong>Paths to used projects</strong> list.<br /> </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="use_path_variables_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br />If you do not want your modeling tool to automatically display the <span class="hcp1"><strong>Use Path Variables</strong></span> dialog box, clear the <span class="hcp1"><strong>Show suggestion to use path variables</strong></span> check box. To use the original path and cancel this dialog, click the <span class="hcp1"><strong>Use Original</strong> button.</span></p><p><span class="hcp1"> </span></p><p><span class="hcp1">To use a suggested path variable</span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="c883082f-86d9-40ee-b6d0-7d88b3428d91">In the <span class="hcp1"><strong>Available forms</strong></span> list, select a path and click the <span class="hcp1"><strong>Use Selected</strong> button.</span></li></ul><p><span class="hcp1"> </span></p><p>If you have your own documentation running on the local server, you can define the path in the <em>documentation.server</em> option.</p><p />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732309 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02791: (2026x) Creating pins automatically for Opaque Action

- page_id: `272732309`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732309/2026x+Creating+pins+automatically+for+Opaque+Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:25.948+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Working with model elements in SysML v1
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Activity Diagram' space=''], you can create an Opaque Action and define a formula. The inputs and output should be shown as pins on that Opaque Action. You can automatically create pins.

To create Pins automatically using the **Parse and Create Pin**button

1. Create a new Opaque Action shape or select an existing one.
2. Type a constraint expression or a new variable for an existing expression.
3. Click the shape and from the smart manipulator toolbar, select [ATTACHMENT filename='parse_and_create_pins.png'] .
4. Pins are created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### On top border of the Opaque Action, the input pins x and y are created, and on the bottom border, the output pin is created.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML Activity Diagram" /></ac:link>, you can create an Opaque Action and define a formula. The inputs and output should be shown as pins on that Opaque Action. You can automatically create pins.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create Pins automatically using the <strong>Parse and Create Pin </strong>button</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a new Opaque Action shape or select an existing one.</li><li>Type a constraint expression or a new variable for an existing expression.</li><li>Click the shape and from the smart manipulator toolbar, select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parse_and_create_pins.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating pins automatically for Opaque Action" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Pins are created.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="parse_and_create_pins_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating pins automatically for Opaque Action" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">On top border of the Opaque Action, the input pins x and y are created, and on the bottom border, the output pin is created.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731100 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02792: (2026x) Creating ports

- page_id: `272731100`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731100/2026x+Creating+ports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:35.993+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x) Defining interfaces using ports
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create the following kinds of ports by using the SysML v1 Plugin:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'] - expose features of the owning Block or its internal Part Properties.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED'] - support their own features.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED'] - since it is deprecated, you use the Proxy Port typed by the Interface Block instead of the non-atomic Flow Port.

##### Creating ports

The procedure below is suitable for all kinds of ports.

To create a port, do one of the following:

- From the diagram palette, select the Proxy Port , Full Port , or Flow Port button and click the element on the diagram pane.
- Select an element on the diagram pane and from the smart manipulator toolbar, click the Proxy Port , Full Port , or Flow Port button.
- In the Block [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''] , select the Ports/Interfaces property group, click the Create button, and select Proxy Port , Full Port , or Flow Port button.

You can also create[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED']at both ends of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED']by dragging the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED']. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Connecting parts through interface' space='']>]]>

##### Converting the Part Property to Full Port

A Full Port is a part on a boundary. You can convert your Parts Properties into Full Ports by dragging them to the diagram frame.

#### INFO: Note

Note

All ports, connectors, and other information, including the layout, will remain unchanged.

To convert a Part Property to the Full Port

1. Select the Part Property shape and drag it to the diagram frame.
2. When the diagram frame is highlighted in blue, drop the shape. 
The Full Port is created on the diagram frame. NoteWhen the Full Port is created on the diagram frame, you cannot drag it back (except to Undo the conversion).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Part Property converted to the Full Port.

##### Creating a port while creating a Connector

The procedure below is suitable for all kinds of ports.

To create a port automatically when drawing a Connector from a port to a Part Property

1. Select a port shape on the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. Select Part Property shape appears. The menu appears if a new port can be created on the Part Property or it has hidden ports.
3. From the Select Port menu, select one of the following commands:

- None - if you want to connect the Connector straight to the part.
- On a port in the list. Hidden ports of the part are listed. The selected port will be represented on the diagram and the connector will be connected to it.
- New Port - to create a new port with the same name, type, and multiplicity as the port from which the connector is drawn.
- New Nested Port - to create a new nested port. Note that the command exists only if the connector is drawn from the nested port.

You can draw a Connector to a port, too, and the menu appears. As a result, the nested port is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The **Select Port** menu opens after drawing the Connector from the *CANPort* Port to Part Property typed by *GPS Module* Block.

**Related pages**

- Displaying parts and ports
- Connecting parts through interface
- Layout templates
- Refactoring
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED']

**Port features**

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures on this page is the **diagram aspects** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\diagram aspects.mdzip.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735147 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02793: (2026x) Creating Pr diagram

- page_id: `272735147`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735147/2026x+Creating+Pr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:56.073+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) Pr Configuration Management
- labels: ['create-pr', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create Pr diagram

truenone

##### Create new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in Pr diagram

1. Click the **Add New** button and select Version of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select **Resource**.
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Assignment Wizard' space=''] .
3. Click Finish when you are done. The resource is assigned for Version Of Configuration in specified time frame. [ATTACHMENT filename='version_of_configuration.png']

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736164 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02794: (2026x) Creating predefined relation map

- page_id: `272736164`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736164/2026x+Creating+predefined+relation+map
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:48.854+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing traceability and impact analysis > (2026x) Traceability relation maps
- labels: ['create-predefined-relation-map', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create predefined relation maps for the selected context elements.

To create the predefined relation map for the context element, do either

- Right-click the element, select Create Diagram and under the UAF Analysis Diagrams , and choose the relation map you want to create.
- Right-click the element, select Related Elements and then choose the relation map you want to create.

You can also create the predefined relation maps in the following way:

- In the main toolbar, click the Create Diagram button, under the UAF Analysis Diagrams group, choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create predefined relation maps for the selected context elements.</p><p>To create the predefined relation map for the context element, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the element, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">and under the </span> <strong>UAF Analysis Diagrams</strong>, and choose the relation map you want to create.</li><li>Right-click the element, select <strong>Related Elements</strong> and then choose the relation map you want to create.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac9b2656-f75a-48e1-9ee5-3f42d303776d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also create the predefined relation maps in the following way:</p><ul><li>In the main toolbar, click the<strong> Create Diagram</strong> button, under the UAF Analysis Diagrams group, choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739468 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02795: (2026x) Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams

- page_id: `272739468`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739468/2026x+Creating+Process+Flow+Diagrams+From+Compositions+or+Aggregations+Defined+in+Process+Diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:24.472+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1179127095

INLINE

1179127116

INLINE

1179127105

INLINE

When you have a process diagram with the defined aggregation/composition of activities/functions, you can create a process flow diagram for the selected activity/function:

- From the Smart manipulator toolbar.
- From the shortcut menu in the Containment tree.

To create a process flow diagram

1. Do one of the following:
  - In a process diagram pane, click the activity for which you want to create a process flow diagram. In the Smart manipulator toolbar, click the process flow diagram button. [ATTACHMENT filename='process_to_process_flow.png']
  - In the Containment tree, right-click the activity and in the shortcut menu, click Create Diagram and the choose <*view*> Process Flow .
2. The Select Behavior dialog opens. In the open dialog, select which behaviors you want to be created in the process flow diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_behavior_dialog.png']
3. Click OK .

The new process flow diagram is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Note, that only the first level behaviors will be suggested in the **Select Behavior** dialog.

1179127084

INLINE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732003 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02797: (2026x) Creating projects for Ecore modeling

- page_id: `272732003`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732003/2026x+Creating+projects+for+Ecore+modeling
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:01.976+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Ecore support
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305881821

305881823

305881822

You can use the following ways to create a project for Ecore modeling: 
 
To create a new project from the Ecore template

1. From the main menu, select **File**> **New Project**. The **New Project** dialog opens.
2. In the **Other** domain, select **Project from Template**.
3. Type a project name.
4. Specify the project location.
5. Under **Select template**, expand **Metamodeling** and then select **Ecore Template**.
6. Click **OK** when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 
To apply the Ecore profile to a project

The Ecore profile can be applied only to existing UML or CMOF / EMOF projects.

1. From the main menu, select **File**> **Use Project...** The **Use Project** wizard opens.
2. Under **Select a project to use**, click **From predefined location**. The **Paths to used projects** list appears.
3. Select <*install root>\profiles*. The content of the <*install root>\profiles* folder is listed bellow.
4. Select *Ecore_Profile.xml* in the list.
5. Click **Next**, if you want to change used projects usage settings.
6. Click **Finish**.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 If your Ecore model references some standard Ecore elements (such as standard data types (for example, EShort) or standard metaclasses (for example, EStructuralFeature), you need to use the standard Ecore library in your project. 
 
To use the standard Ecore library in a project

1. From the main menu, select **File**> **Use Project...** The **Use Project** wizard opens.
2. Under **Select a project to use**, click **From predefined location**. The **Paths to used projects** list appears.
3. Select <*installroot>\modelLibraries*. The content of the <*install root>\modelLibraries* folder is listed bellow.
4. Select *Ecore.mdzip* in the list.
5. Click **Next**, if you want to change used projects usage settings.
6. Click **Finish**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

305881820

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating new projects from templates' space='']

****
