<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739395 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02798: (2026x) Creating PV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272739395`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739395/2026x+Creating+PV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:20.691+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Project viewpoint > (2026x) PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['create-pv-1-diagram', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] before creating a PV-1.

To create a PV-1 diagram

1. Create Actual Projects and Actual Organizations.
2. Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] ) or Actual Posts ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] ) responsible for them using Responsible For relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating a PV-1.</p><p>To create a PV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Projects and Actual Organizations.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects with Actual  Organizations (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Posts </span>(<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) responsible for them using <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Responsible For </span>relationship.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739401 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02799: (2026x) Creating PV-1 matrix

- page_id: `272739401`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739401/2026x+Creating+PV-1+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:21.069+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Project viewpoint > (2026x) PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['create-pv-1-matrix', 'build-pv-1-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] before creating a PV-1.

The PV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of actual organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, andActual Posts) for Projects.

The rows in the PV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent actual organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts) and the columns represent Actual Projects .

To create a PV-1 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Projects).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating a PV-1.</p><p>The PV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of actual organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Actual Posts</span>) for Projects.</p><p>The rows in the PV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent actual organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts) and the columns represent Actual Projects .</p><p>To create a PV-1 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739399 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02800: (2026x) Creating PV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272739399`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739399/2026x+Creating+PV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:20.949+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Project viewpoint > (2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines
- labels: ['create-pv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create PV-2 diagram, the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a PV-2 diagram

1. Right-click the PV-2 Package and select Create Diagram > PV-2 Project Timelines .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create PV-2 Project Timelines** check box.

To create a PV-2 diagram manually when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** is disabled

1. Right-click the PV-2 Package and select Create Diagram > PV-2 Project Timelines .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter . The blank PV-2 Project Timelines diagram is created.

To restore the usage of the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you choose to create PV-2 diagram, the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body> <strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</p><p>To create a PV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the PV-2 Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>PV-2 Project Timelines</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c77ed02-f212-4ece-8767-9903e864e6c7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create PV-2 Project Timelines</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a PV-2 diagram manually when <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the PV-2 Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>PV-2 Project Timelines</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press <strong>Enter</strong>. <br />The blank <strong>PV-2 Project Timelines</strong> diagram is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be5825d5-3d47-4481-9536-da3fe0194dee"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the<strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[PV-2 Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UAF</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard</strong> <strong>each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to<em> true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739796 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02801: (2026x) Creating PV-3 matrix

- page_id: `272739796`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739796/2026x+Creating+PV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:58.485+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Project viewpoint > (2026x) PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping
- labels: ['create-pv-3-matrix', 'build-pv-3-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping** describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.

The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.

To create a PV-3 matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Actual Projects).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix.

By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:

- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.

You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the **Specification** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping</strong> describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability. </p><p>The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a PV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix. </p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:</span></p><ul><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b006062-f431-4f55-b590-c1c8054e6b8b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping_criteria.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating PV-3 matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729430 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02802: (2026x) Creating relation map

- page_id: `272729430`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729430/2026x+Creating+relation+map
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:35:15.050+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) Analysis diagrams > (2026x) Relation Map
- labels: ['create-relation-map', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a relation map

1. Select an element, for which you need to create the relation map. This element is the context of the relation map.
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram . Type “rel” and press Enter .
  - On the main toolbar, click the Create Diagram button. Type “rel” and press Enter .
  - Press Ctrl+N. Type “rel” and press Enter .
  - On the Analyze menu, click Create Relation Map .
  - Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select Create Diagram > Analysis Diagrams > Relation Map .
  - Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select Related Elements > Create Relation Map .

The newly created relation map opens on the right side of the application window.

- The context of the relation map automatically becomes the owner of it when using any of the last two approaches.
- When using other approaches, you can specify another element as the owner.
- The actions relevant for diagrams are valid for relation maps as well.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a relation map</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, for which you need to create the relation map. This element is the context of the relation map.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Diagrams</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Diagram</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. Type “rel” and press </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Enter</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>On the main toolbar, click the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Diagram</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> button. Type “rel” and press </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Enter</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Press Ctrl+N. Type “rel” and press <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Enter</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>On the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Analyze</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> menu, click </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Relation Map</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Diagram</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt;</span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Analysis Diagrams</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Relation Map</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Related Elements</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt;</span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Relation Map</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li></ul></li></ol><p>The newly created relation map opens on the right side of the application window.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="198500c2-d1fb-45cd-bbf8-9975f8f67040"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The context of the relation map automatically becomes the owner of it when using any of the last two approaches.</li><li>When using other approaches, you can specify another element as the owner.</li><li>The actions relevant for diagrams are valid for relation maps as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738085 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02803: (2026x) Creating Requirement Diagram for sub-requirements

- page_id: `272738085`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738085/2026x+Creating+Requirement+Diagram+for+sub-requirements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:35.974+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Requirements management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create a Requirements Diagram for sub-requirements of the selected requirement directly on the diagram pane.

To create a Requirements Diagram for sub-requirements

1. Select a Requirement shape on the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click the Create diagram for sub-requirements button.

A new requirements diagram for the sub-requirements is created, having the same name as the selected requirement. The selected requirement is the owner of the sub-requirements diagram and is marked with the diagram sign.

Double-click the owner symbol to open a diagram for sub-requirements and use navigation buttons on the diagram toolbar to move back or forward through diagrams.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create a Requirements Diagram for sub-requirements of the selected requirement directly on the diagram pane.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Requirements Diagram for sub-requirements</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Requirement shape on the diagram pane.</li><li>On the smart manipulator, click the <strong>Create diagram for sub-requirements</strong> button.</li></ol><p>A new requirements diagram for the sub-requirements is created, having the same name as the selected requirement. The selected requirement is the owner of the sub-requirements diagram and is marked with the diagram sign.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7cdcdd2-5841-4506-98d0-037b50f64bd5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Double-click the owner symbol to open a diagram for sub-requirements and use navigation buttons on the diagram toolbar to move back or forward through diagrams.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738031 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02804: (2026x) Creating requirements

- page_id: `272738031`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738031/2026x+Creating+requirements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:28.063+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Requirements management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create and represent text-based requirements and relate them to other modeling elements. The requirements can be depicted in graphical, tabular, or tree structure format. This page provides instructions for creating requirements in the model. You can create requirements in the:

- 
- .
- .

##### containmentCreating requirements in the Containment tree

If you create Requirements in the Containment tree, the element is created in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space='']. After that you can represent them on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space=''] by dragging them or in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Requirement Table' space=''] by adding an existing element.

To create requirements in the Containment tree

1. Open an existing or create a new Requirement Diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']]]>
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Containment tab' space=''] , right-click the package, or another Requirement where you want to create or nest a new Requirement.
3. From the shortcut menu, click Create Element .
4. In the **Create Element** dialog, select any type of Requirement. If you have created your own custom Requirement types, they appear under the**Custom Requirements** group.
5. Type a Requirement name. The new requirement is created in the Model Browser.

##### diagramCreating requirements in the Requirement diagram

If you create Requirements directly on the diagram pane, the element is created in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space='']as well.

To create Requirements on the Requirement Diagram pane

1. Open an existing or create a new Requirement Diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']]]>
2. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space=''], select a **Requirement** or other type of requirement button and click on the diagram pane. The element is created in the same package where the diagram is stored.
3. On the Requirement shape, type a name, ID, and description. The Requirement is created and added in the table.

You can find more information about how to work in diagrams on the page [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagramming' space=''].

To select a different owner

1. Hold **Shift**, click**Add New** button, and select a Requirement type from the drop-down menu. 
The **Select Owner** dialog opens, enabling you to choose a different owner.

##### tableCreating requirements in the Requirement Table

You can create new, nested requirements, or add existing ones in the Requirement Table.

###### Creating new Requirements

To create a new Requirement in a Requirement Table

1. Open an existing or create a new Requirement Table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']]]>
2. On the Requirement table toolbar, click the Add New button or press Insert ( Ctrl+I for Mac users). The list of Requirement types opens.
3. Click a desired requirement type. A new line above the first numbered requirement appears.
4. In that empty line, type a requirement name and specify other requirement properties. To select a different owner1. Hold **Shift**, click**Add New** button, and select a Requirement type from the drop-down menu. 
The **Select Owner** dialog opens, enabling you to choose a different owner.

###### Adding existing Requirements

To add existing Requirements in a Requirement Table

1. Open an existing or create a new Requirement Table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']]]>
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Requirement table toolbar, click the Add Existing button or press Ctrl + Insert ( Ctrl+I for Mac users). In the Select Requirement dialog, select the requirements you want to represent in the table and click OK .
  - Drag the entire requirements Package from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space=''] and drop it on the empty Requirement table to represent all its content in the table.

###### Adding nested Requirements

To add nested Requirement in the Requirement Table

1. Select Requirement you want to nest in the table.
2. Click the Add Nested button or press Alt+Insert ( Alt+I for Mac users).
3. Select the desired Requirement type from the drop-down menu. If you have created your own custom Requirement types, they appear under the Custom Requirements group in the drop-down menu.

More information about how to work in tables you can find in the following pages:

- Table toolbars
- Table Criteria area
- Basic tasks in tables

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Dragging objects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Relating requirements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Table toolbars' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Table Criteria area' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Basic tasks in tables' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can create and represent text-based requirements and relate them to other modeling elements. The requirements can be depicted in graphical, tabular, or tree structure format. This page provides instructions for creating requirements in the model. You can create requirements in the:</p><ul><li data-uuid="24c8e9f1-c587-4f2c-8115-a857222350ee"><ac:link ac:anchor="containment"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="faac0c5e-dfd5-4493-9eaf-f9e97e216bcb"><ac:link ac:anchor="diagram"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Requirement Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="9cd45c4f-c484-4656-838f-88e89c679b13"><ac:link ac:anchor="table"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Requirement Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8237217a-aef1-4047-a44c-f957fe84ee2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">containment</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating requirements in the Containment tree</h3><p>If you create Requirements in the Containment tree, the element is created in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. data-uuid="717f333d-b508-4b57-95d5-084fcb8b868a"><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pallet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, select a <strong>Requirement</strong> or other type of requirement button and click on the diagram pane.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01bb1960-e357-4ecc-84b4-d3c5a623f2b3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The element is created in the same package where the diagram is stored.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="2f09b5bf-7591-4414-a782-989613534492">On the Requirement shape, type a name, ID, and description. <br />The Requirement is created and added in the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7a2f973-09ca-45d8-b443-72d396a20f4e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can find more information about how to work in diagrams on the page <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagramming" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe06ca89-4a1d-4f06-8848-9aafb5c28174"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To select a different owner</p><hr /><p>1. Hold <strong>Shift</strong>, click<strong> Add New</strong> button, and select a Requirement type from the drop-down menu.<br />The <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog opens, enabling you to choose a different owner.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ddd2de8-c294-412e-aa9e-200266fc5f90"><ac:parameter ac:name="">table</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating requirements in the Requirement Table</h3><p>You can create new, nested requirements, or add existing ones in the Requirement Table.</p><h4>Creating new Requirements</h4><p>To create a new Requirement in a Requirement Table</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="77fe386e-93e9-4d70-b110-dd3e5b106cf3">Open an existing or create a new Requirement Table. <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a table >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="e901a71e-7818-4025-b246-05c5c7938fec">On the Requirement table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button or press Insert (<strong>Ctrl+I</strong> for Mac users). The list of Requirement types opens.</li><li data-uuid="90d5bfba-47d1-449a-86df-d0b49c16c411">Click a desired requirement type. A new line above the first numbered requirement appears.</li><li data-uuid="e6798e0b-bd36-44dd-8dde-a06b66c8a393"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In that empty line, type a requirement name and specify other requirement properties.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="502f83f1-7535-48ea-a85a-6ef13aacf04f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To select a different owner</p><hr /><p>1. Hold <strong>Shift</strong>, click<strong> Add New</strong> button, and select a Requirement type from the drop-down menu.<br />The <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog opens, enabling you to choose a different owner.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Adding existing Requirements</h4><p>To add existing Requirements in a Requirement Table</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="76414743-e991-4af8-a87f-1b027b71a6b7">Open an existing or create a new Requirement Table. <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a table >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="d794581b-4add-4f5a-9163-7764867d2e45">Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Requirement table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button or press Ctrl + Insert (<strong>Ctrl+I</strong> for Mac users). In the <strong>Select Requirement</strong> dialog, select the requirements you want to represent in the table and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag the entire requirements Package from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and drop it on the empty Requirement table to represent all its content in the table.<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ol><h4>Adding nested Requirements</h4><p>To add nested Requirement in the Requirement Table</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="750d1ae9-5243-4373-81e1-31f7caceb78e">Select Requirement you want to nest in the table.</li><li data-uuid="43d298b3-f339-44aa-be90-70acd867e513">Click the <strong>Add Nested</strong> button or press <strong>Alt+Insert </strong>(<strong>Alt+I </strong>for Mac users).</li><li data-uuid="cd55b033-7626-4bed-8653-bfc86617af8f"><p>Select the desired Requirement type from the drop-down menu.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec84a738-898f-4f40-ad05-548e092fffdd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have created your own custom Requirement types, they appear under the Custom Requirements group in the drop-down menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><br /></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2165c4db-3c9c-47e9-a6c7-057659d50b24"><ac:rich-text-body><p>More information about how to work in tables you can find in the following pages:</p><ul><li data-uuid="4a4bd1c9-d188-48a7-a2a4-1a18a698b942"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Table+toolbars">Table toolbars</a></li><li data-uuid="25b9a4dc-6397-4aa2-a4e2-8ef536e36364"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Table+Criteria+area">Table Criteria area</a></li><li data-uuid="1b12d619-4791-420f-8889-7b440f8e626b"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Basic+tasks+in+tables">Basic tasks in tables</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="81a9d168-92f0-480c-803f-bfe7f4da9af5"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="dc96f2f8-c327-492d-90b6-fa092306fd18"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Opening diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="bd8142b0-7a49-40f2-a9c1-05fc328c19c3"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dragging objects" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="4427a71d-9da9-4dee-96d7-39f23acbc887"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Relating requirements" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="b5a53259-2f4c-4731-b1fd-5ed0e8c161be"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Table toolbars" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="ac67d7e9-6e87-49f6-bcb2-38062cd3309d"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Table Criteria area" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="dc952579-c7ac-4fc5-861e-298cee688b7d"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Basic tasks in tables" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738021 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02805: (2026x) Creating Requirements Project

- page_id: `272738021`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738021/2026x+Creating+Requirements+Project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:29.417+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Requirements management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create requirements using the the Requirements Project template. It contains predefined packages, elements, and diagrams to start creating your own requirements in the model.

The Requirements Project template is only available in MagicDraw with Cameo Requirement Modeler Plugin installed.

To create a Requirement Project

1. Open the New Project dialog. Do one of the following:
  - 
    - From the File menu, select New Project .
    - On the main toolbar, click the New Project button.
    - Press Ctrl + Shift + N.
2. In the New Project dialog, specify the following:
  - On the left side of the dialog, under the General-Purpose Modeling domain, select the Requirements Project .
  - On the right side of the dialog, type the file name in the Name box.
  - On the right side of the dialog, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='select_location_button.png'] button and select the location to store the created project. [ATTACHMENT filename='requirements_projects_templates.png']
3. Click OK . The new Requirement project is created with predefined structure as shown in the image below. [ATTACHMENT filename='requirements_project_structure.png']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with projects' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li class="atl-forced-newline">From the <strong>File</strong> menu, select <strong>New Project</strong>.</li><li <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731175 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02806: (2026x) Creating Resource  Exchange in MODAF SV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272731175`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731175/2026x+Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+MODAF+SV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:32.350+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in SV-1 diagram

1. Open the Resource Exchange creation wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Interface and on the smart manipulator, click New Resource Exchange .
  - Select the Association and on the smart manipulator, click New Resource Exchange .
  - In the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange and connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. In the Resource Exchange creation wizard specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.
3. Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource Exchange in SV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard by doing one of the following:<ul><li>Select the Resource Interface and on the smart manipulator, click <strong>New Resource Exchange </strong>.</li><li>Select the Association and on the smart manipulator, click <strong>New Resource Exchange </strong>.</li><li>In the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>and connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.</li><li>Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731182 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02807: (2026x) Creating Resource Exchange in MODAF SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272731182`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731182/2026x+Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+MODAF+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:34.506+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. SV-2 Systems Communications Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in SV-2 diagrams

1. Open the Resource Exchange creation wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Interface and in the smart manipulator, click New Resource Exchange .
  - On the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange and then click the Resource Interface.
  - On the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange button and then connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. In the Resource Exchange creation wizard, specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.
3. Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource Exchange in SV-2 diagrams</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard by doing one of the following:<ul><li>Select the Resource Interface and in the smart manipulator, click <strong>New Resource Exchange</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>and then click the Resource Interface.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>button and then connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard, specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.</li><li>Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735003 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02809: (2026x) Creating Resource Exchange in P1 diagram

- page_id: `272735003`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735003/2026x+Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+P1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:42.688+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-p1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in P1 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Association and on the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - In the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and connect the Resources on the diagram pane.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

/><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Select the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Association</span> and on the smart manipulator, click <ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange" ac:alt="New Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /></span></li><li>In the diagram palette, click <ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange" ac:alt="Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and connect the Resources on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:</span><ul><li>Command (between Organizational Resources)</li><li>Control (between Physical Resources)</li><li>Resource Exchange</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d43f9522-3e3d-4a70-8a85-739ccc6a013c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0d895a14-702c-4420-8c23-20e1efdc794e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span class="confluence-link">You can use <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link">Resource Exchange Manager</span> </strong>dialog</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

## PAGE 02810: (2026x) Creating Resource Exchange in P2i diagram

To create a Resource Exchangein P2i diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Exchange or Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and then click the Resource Connector.
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and then connect the Resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735124 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02811: (2026x) Creating Resource Exchange in P4 Resource Functions diagram

- page_id: `272735124`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735124/2026x+Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+P4+Resource+Functions+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:52.010+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-p4-resource-functions', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Resource Exchangein the P4 Resource Functions diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow and on the smart manipulator toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and then click the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow .
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the ResourceExchangesdata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an <span class="confluence-link">Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>in the P4 Resource Functions diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Function <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Control Flow or Function Object Flow</span></span> and on the smart manipulator toolbar click <ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange " ac:alt="New Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click <ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange " ac:alt="Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then click the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Function <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Control Flow or Function Object Flow</span></span>.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Exchange</span>, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</span></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf9b0a26-2235-4e72-9891-8e64840a4a65"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use<span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Exchange</span> Manager</strong></span> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> that enables to analyze and manage the <span class="confluence-link">Resource </span><span class="confluence-link" style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 02812: (2026x) Creating Resource Exchange in P6 diagram

- page_id: `272735136`
- space_key: `MT`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchangein P6 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**RealizedResource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and then click the Resource Message.
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and connect the Resource lifeline elements on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

Direction.</span></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ## PAGE 02813: (2026x) Creating Resource Exchange in SV-1 Internal diagram

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchangein SV-1 Internal diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click the Resource Connector.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>in SV-1 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and then click the Resource Connector.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</span></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="135f259e-df6b-4a88-826a-382a79531cef"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739915 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02815: (2026x) Creating Resource Exchange in SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272739915`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739915/2026x+Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:12.733+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchangein SV-2 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, connect resources.
  - Click the Control relationship and connect Posts or Organizations with Posts, Organizations, Natural Resources, Software, Resource Artifact, Technology.
  - Click the Command relationship and connect Posts, Organizations.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>in SV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Association  </span>between a pair of resources and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange" ac:alt="New Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange" ac:alt="Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and on the diagram pane, connect<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> resources.</span></li><li>Click the Control relationship and connect Posts or Organizations with Posts, Organizations, Natural Resources, Software, Resource Artifact, Technology.</li><li>Click the Command relationship and connect Posts, Organizations.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edd9cc73-f3d1-4686-a619-303c55fccc6e"><ac:rich-text-body>Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2aa563e-be67-452b-ae59-f97ff2723412"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span class="confluence-link">You can use <span class="confluence-link">Resource Exchange Manager</span> </strong>dialog</span></span> </ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731662 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02817: (2026x) Creating Resource Exchange in SV-3 matrix

- page_id: `272731662`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731662/2026x+Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+SV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:30.969+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix
- labels: ['add-resource-interaction-in-sv-3-matrix', 'delete-resource-interaction-from-sv-3-matrix', 'manage-sv-3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new Resource Exchange

1. Right-click the intersection in the matrix and select Add New Resource Exchange .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard .
3. Click Finish when you are done. A new Resource Exchange appears in the selected intersection.

To remove an existing Resource Exchange

1. Right-click the filled intersection.
2. Under the Delete section, choose the Resource Exchange you want to remove from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a new Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the intersection in the matrix and select <strong>Add New Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />A new Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>appears in the selected intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the filled intersection.</li><li>Under the <strong>Delete</strong> section, choose the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>you want to remove from the model.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-4-systems-functionality-description', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Resource Exchangein the SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow and on the smart manipulator toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the ResourceExchangesdata.

## PAGE 02819: (2026x) Creating Resource Exchange in SvcV-10c diagram

- page_id: `272739984`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739984/2026x+Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+SvcV-10c+diagram
- version_number: 1

- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-svcv-10c', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchangein SvcV-10c diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**RealizedResource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and then click the Resource Message.
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and connect the Resource lifeline elements on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732873 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02820: (2026x) Creating Resource Exchanges in NSV-2 diagram

- page_id: `272732873`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732873/2026x+Creating+Resource+Exchanges+in+NSV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:22.769+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x) NSV-2 System Communications Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in NSV-2 diagrams

1. Open the Resource Exchange creation wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Interface and in the smart manipulator, click New Resource Exchange .
  - On the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange and then click the Resource Interface.
  - On the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange button and then connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. In the Resource Exchange creation wizard, specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.
3. Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource Exchange in NSV-2 diagrams</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard by doing one of the following:<ul><li>Select the Resource Interface and in the smart manipulator, click <strong>New Resource Exchange</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>and then click the Resource Interface.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>button and then connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard, specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.</li><li>Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739988 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02821: (2026x) Creating Resource Exchanges in SV-1 diagram

- page_id: `272739988`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739988/2026x+Creating+Resource+Exchanges+in+SV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:18.582+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchangein SV-1 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and on the diagram pane, connect resources.
  - Click the Control relationship and connect Posts or Organizations with Posts, Organizations, Natural Resources, Software, Resource Artifact, Technology.
  - Click the Command relationship and connect Posts, Organizations.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog **** to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

****
