<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732191 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02822: (2026x) Creating reusable constraint libraries

- page_id: `272732191`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732191/2026x+Creating+reusable+constraint+libraries
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:20.947+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling parametric constraints
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add or create constraints in a Package, such as a library, and export it as a new project, so that you can reuse the library in other projects.

The following sections present instructions on how to export a package as a new project and how to work with shared packages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exporting packages to new projects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Sharing project data' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add or create constraints in a Package, such as a library, and export it as a new project, so that you can reuse the library in other projects.</p><p>The following sections present instructions on how to export a package as a new project and how to work with shared packages:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exporting packages to new projects" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Sharing project data" /></ac:link></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733926 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02823: (2026x) Creating S1 diagram

- page_id: `272733926`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733926/2026x+Creating+S1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:13.203+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Service viewpoint > (2026x) S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-s1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create S1 just before finishing an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L1 Node Types' space=''] or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) P1 Resource Types' space='']. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.

To create an S1 diagram

1. Create Service Interfaces.
2. Connect Service Interfaces using Generalizations (general-specific relationships).

The S1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create S1 just before finishing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L1 Node Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1 view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.</p><p>To create an S1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Interfaces.</li><li>Connect Service Interfaces using Generalizations (general-specific relationships).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2d16256-bd5e-4a9e-b2f3-1e818e46fc67"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The S1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734901 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02824: (2026x) Creating S2 diagram

- page_id: `272734901`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734901/2026x+Creating+S2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:38.308+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Service viewpoint > (2026x) S2 Service Structure
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an S2 Service Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the S2 package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**S2 Service Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for**S2 Service Structure,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Service element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create a Service in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Service and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Service in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the needed package, and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Service and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_drag_service.png']

Service

To create the elements from other resources (e.g., Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Service (or another appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connecting the Services

When a Service is created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the ServiceRoles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity are shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element that will be the Aggregation End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Directed Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_edit_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''].

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_specify_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the Specification window of the role element.

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. Using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying related elements' space=''] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying related elements' space='']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_display_related.png']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Select the element and click the Compartments, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Smart manipulators' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_compartment.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such a case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Smart manipulators' space=''] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying parts and ports' space=''] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying parts and ports' space=''] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_display_parts.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an S2 Service Structure diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the S2 package and do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> S2 Service Structure</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="170" ac:width="310"><ri:attachment ri:filename="s2_new_diagram)containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for<strong> S2 Service Structure, </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Service element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.<br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Service in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Service and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="244" ac:width="562"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_create_service.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Service in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right-click the needed package, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Service and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="278" ac:width="323"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_new_service_from_containment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="220" ac:width="761"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_drag_service.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7d01530-058c-4324-a12b-1096609019f2"><ac:rich-text-body>The structure of each <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service </span>can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) S2 Service Internal Structure" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g., Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service </span>(or another appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connecting the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Services</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When a Service is created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To connect the elements with the Composition relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the Smart Manipulator, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> which appears after you select the element, choose</span> Directed Composition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0780fc43-c960-4eb5-907e-da21eeb36ba3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_direct_composition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="306" ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_connect_services.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service </span>Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity are shown.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="298" ac:width="517"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element that will be the Aggregation End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Association</strong> and choose <strong>Directed Aggregation</strong></span>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="368" ac:width="387"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_directed_aggregation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="172" ac:width="526"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_edit_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="711" ac:width="494"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_specify_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the Specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Displaying created structure</h3><p>If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. Using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8fe10c2-93e5-4905-aae2-8a0324b428b4"><ac:rich-text-body>If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the <strong>Resource Association</strong> also.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="882" ac:width="578"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_display_related.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.</li><li>Select the element and click the Compartments, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_compartment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="711a7e71-ce7a-474a-a540-955126beae32"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such a case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="786" ac:width="770"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_display_parts.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734924 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02825: (2026x) Creating S4 Service Functions diagram

- page_id: `272734924`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734924/2026x+Creating+S4+Service+Functions+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:38.791+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Service viewpoint > (2026x) S4 Service Functions
- labels: ['create-s4-service-functions', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an S4 Service Functions diagram

1. Create Service Function Actions or drag the them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Service Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an S4 Service Functions diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Function Actions or drag the them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Service Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734922 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02826: (2026x) Creating S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `272734922`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734922/2026x+Creating+S4+Service+Functions+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:38.703+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Service viewpoint > (2026x) S4 Service Functions
- labels: ['create-s4-service-functions-taxonomy', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Use Compositions or Aggregations (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions or Aggregations (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734929 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02827: (2026x) Creating S8 table

- page_id: `272734929`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734929/2026x+Creating+S8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:39.162+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Service viewpoint > (2026x) S8 Service Policy
- labels: ['create-s8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create S8 table, do either

- Add a new Service Policy by clicking the Add New button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL).
- Add an existing Service Policy by clicking the Add Existing button and select a Service Policies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create S8 table<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">, do either</span><span> </span></p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Service Policy by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL).</li><li>Add an existing Service Policy by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select a Service Policies.</li></ul>
````
